BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/26:

- As expected, 3rd-year TB RB Bucky Irving is NOT present on the field at OTAs as he is still recovering and rehabbing on injured shoulder that required surgery. HC Todd Bowles when asked about Irvings return: "Sometime in the summer or fall". 😕
- TEN QB Cam Ward on 1st-Rd Rookie Carnell Tate after first week of OTAs: "He'll have an explosive year just because he'll get a lot of one-on-one matchups. That's what we want. We want guys to come down and play, man, because we think we can beat him." 🚀
- 3rd-Year HOU TE Cade Stover has begun taking reps at FB while also serving as TE depth for the Texans. The Ohio State product has yet to play a full season due to injuries and is looking for a way to remain on the roster after cut-down time competing with the likes of Brevin Jordan, Foster Moreau, and Harrison Bryant.
- RUMOR MILL: Could Veteran SF WR Brandon Aiyuk be a low-risk, high-reward match to the New York Giants, who could possibly be without Malik Nabers to begin the 2026 season?
-  NY Jets want to give rookie QB Cade Klubnik a "good look and evaluation" before making any final decisions on signing a back-up veteran QB behind starter Geno Smith.
- Bear's QB Coach J.T. Barrett on emphasis for year three for starting QB Caleb Williams: "More efficiency, Less Superhero Plays". 
- Pelissero expects "fair competition going in" for the Minnesota QBs Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy heading into training camp. “If this were just, it’s Kyler’s job, and J.J. is competing with Carson Wentz to be the two, you would have traded J.J."
- Per @AlbertBreer: Chiefs, Chargers, Bills and Rams all had their shot at this already at AJ Brown and are all likely out." Stating the obvious also, what is most likely scenario is a reunion with Mike Vrabel.
- Raider's HC Klint Kubiak reiterates once again after second day of OTAs pertaining to 2nd year stud RB Ashton Jeanty on a potential snap split in the backfield: "I don't know the play snap percentage, but you look at Christian McCaffrey, his play snap percentage is high. So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀
- CAR RB Jonathon Brooks participating and looks to be a full go in early OTAs, reports are that he has "bulked up" a little and is already flashing nicely on run and passing game.
- Denver HC Sean Payton was asked "Who is the WR1 between veteran Courtland Sutton and newcomer Jaylen Waddle?", his response: "We’re not just going to have. So, we don’t have [that]. We just play different packages and try to give those guys the things that they do well.”
- The Houston Texans and star WR Nico Collins agreed on a contract negotiation/adjustment that guarantees Collins' salary for the next two NFL seasons, while also getting a pay increase of $8m for this season and $9m for next season. 
- 2nd-Year Jag's WR/CB Travis Hunter is indeed participating in Jaguar's OTAs this week. Was seen doing something quite unspeakable today - catching a pass on a WR route from QB Trevor Lawrence. We thought he only played CB now.
- Jacksonville HC Liam Coen was asked about why newly signed RB Chris Rodriguez Jr. was NOT in attendance for first day of OTAs: "Yeah, you know I mean, the injury report will come out at some point." 
- Per @Schultz_Report, Arizona Cardinals have fielded calls from teams around the league pertaining to trade discussions centered around Pro-Bowl DE/EDGE Josh Sweat.
- 3rd-year TB WR Jalen McMillan on excitement of opening the playbook under new OC Zac Robinson: "We had a lot of plays last year [under Josh Grizzard] that weren't called, but right now we are repping a lot of them and giving the coaches confidence in calling them."
- Jaguar's OC Grant Udinksi gushes over WR Parker Washington in Post-OTA interview: "I think really, really highly of Parker. I hope Parker thinks really, really highly of himself, because we think that he is capable of what he did at the end of the season last year and even more than that." 🚀

BIGGEST NFL News 5/21 (OTAs):

- Fresh off a new mega-max contract extension, Dolphin's star RB DeVon Achane is apparently already dealing with an (undisclosed) injury with absence in Miami's first OTAs segment. 😬
- LAC GM Joe Hortiz reports in interview, "The door is not closed on veteran WR Keenan Allen".
- Titans HC Robert Salah says, "Cam Wards arm is "perfectly healthy".
- Early reports out of Brown's camp is that new HC Todd Monken and veteran QB Deshaun Watson have allegedly "hit it off" early in OTAs. Watson has been taking all of the 1st and 2nd team reps early on.
- Giants HC John Harbaugh reported that WR Darius Slayton recently had surgery for a "sports Hernia" but still feels he will be 100% for the start of training camp.
- KC Chiefs star WR Rashee Rice has been sentenced to a mandatory stay of 30 days in the Dallas County jail for a probation violation due to failing a test for marijuana. 
- KC WR Rashee Rice has "clean up" surgery on his injured right knee to help with inflammation. He will rehab the knee while serving his 30 days in Dallas County jail and stands to be sidelined for roughly 2 months with the rehabbing.
- NYG Stud WR Malik Nabers recently had "clean up" surgery on the same knee he tore his ACL & meniscus during Week 4 of the 2025 NFL season. HC Harbaugh reported, "he is going through the slog of his recovery, and the goal is to still have Nabers out there in Week 1, but it is not a simple recovery". 🙃
- Bills HC Joe Brady reiterates that "Keon Coleman is here to be a part of this offense". Coleman labeled his second year in the NFL as "make or break".
- Veteran QB Kirk Cousins receives ALL of 1st-team reps in Raider's OTAs, followed by Aiden O'Connell, and 3rd-team reps with rookie 1st overall pick Fernando Mendoza. Although, Mendoza has already "stood out".
- 2nd year CLE RB Quinshon Judkins wows Twitter in clip of OTAs catching a pass and quickly turning up field in a dead sprint, looking WELL ahead of his recovery from a broken fibula suffered late last season. 👀
- NYG RB Cam Skattebo tells reporters that he expects to have 300 carries and over 2,000 yards in his 2nd year in the NFL. Skattebo is also recovery from a fractured ankle + fibula and says he will be ready for Week 1. 
- Falcons HC Kevin Stefanski says the 1st team reps being split down the middle for both QBs Tua Tagovailoa and QB Michael Penix in OTAs is "intentional".
- Raven's Star QB Lamar Jackson "not present" at voluntary OTAs on Tuesday.
- Veteran Cardinals QB Jacoby Brissett is not attending Arizona's OTAs, as he is currently seeking a pay raise to be the starting QB for the team in 2026.
- Future HOF QB Aaron Rodgers signs new 1-year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers for up to $25M with incentives. Rodgers reunites with old ball coach Mike McCarthy for what he says will be his final year before retiring. 🙌
- New WAS Commanders OC David Blough persistently states that he wants to get veteran star wide-out Terry McLaurin upwards of 10 targets per game in 2026. 🚀
- The Detroit Lions have officially extended star LB Jack Campbell to a new 4-year contract that will put Campbell with the franchise past 2030.
- High praise from Niners QB Brock Purdy on rookie De'Zhaun Stribling saying, "feel like Stribling is gonna be a guy who comes in and makes an instant impact." 
- Per Adam Schefter, "Tyreek Hill is an injury (Ankle) situation where he might not be ready until midway through the season” 😮
- New Raider's HC Klint Kubiak on 3rd year stud TE Brock Bowers: "He is a football robot from Heaven".
- ATL Falcons 3rd round rookie WR Zachariah Branch has "big day" in first day of open OTAs making "the most explosive play in the Falcons’ offense vs. defense period."
- Titans Beat: Rookie standout WR Carnell Tate INSTANT winner of Titan's first day of OTAs. Citing multiple TD catches from 2nd year QB Cam Ward, working long and short, catching balls all over the field, both open and contested. Looking "as advertised".

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/22:

- Reigning 2025 NFL MVP Matthew Stafford and the LA Rams have agreed on a 1-year contract extension worth up to $60 Million with incentives. Puts Stafford up to nearly 105M remaining on 2 years with the Rams through 2027.
- Rookie CLE WR Denzel Boston makes "play of the day" on a long go-ball from veteran Deshaun Watson resulting in an over-the-shoulder TD grab in Thursday's OTAs.
- Newly acquired BUF WR DJ Moore on his 2026 season outlook: "I am ready to show everybody that I can still do what I do and do it with the Bills." 👀
- 2nd-Year New Orleans QB Tyler Shough on recently 8th overall pick WR Jordyn Tyson: "He's an ELITE separator, going to be VERY friendly to the quarterback!"
- Reports swirling around Buccaneers camp is that year five for HC Todd Bowles is "make or break" in Tampa.
- Chief's GM Brett Veach on QB Patrick Mahomes recovery from ACL/LCL knee injury: "Needless to say, he is well ahead of schedule, but we know the type of competitor Mahomes is and the biggest challenge is protecting him from himself (in terms or moving too fast/doing too much in OTAs)."
- 10-year NYG Veteran DL Roy Robertson-Harris tears Achilles in Thursday's OTAs; will now miss all of the 2026 NFL season. Unfortunate break, as now two DL for an already thin Giants defense are sidelined for this season.
- Jags Beat @_John_Shipley on mailbag question "is CRod the Jag's RB1 over Tuten?": "Think it will be a committee approach and no true RB1. More of a "hot hand" here and there and will use multiple RBs to set the tone. Do think (CRod) gets more carries than Tuten, but not by much."
- New LV Raiders HC Klint Kubiak mirrors same sentiment as 2nd-year Stud RB Ashton Jeanty: "If I don't have to, I don't want to come off the field." Kubiak went on to say, "team wants to put a lot of pressure on [Jeanty]." 🚀
- Rumor Mill: "Rumors surfacing that the KC Chiefs were inquired from the Eagles about a possible trade for A.J. Brown, Chiefs said, "No"." Brown also allegedly gave a list of teams he would prefer to play for including: Patriots, Chiefs, Bills, & Chargers.
- 4th-Year GB TE Tucker Kraft is progressing well in his recovery from a torn ACL suffered in Week 9 of the 2025 NFL season, videos capturing Kraft sprinting and adding resistance for strength training, signs are very positive so far! 👍
- New Eagles OC Sean Mannion on Rookie WR Makai Lemon: “Makai is very impressive, he’s a tremendous young talent, takes a lot of pride in the little detail. With his degree of talent and potential the sky is the limit".
- TEN 2nd-Year QB Cameron Ward has shed 10 pounds during the offseason but watching his calories and upping his cardio regime. Dare we say, "Best shape of his life" SZN is HERE? 😜
- Commanders HC Dan Quinn on rookie WR Antonio Williams: "Has absolutely jumped out. As a route runner, he can stop, he can change directions, explosives, this guy’s got real natural feel as a receiver. Antonio has it, man, in the biggest way.” 🔥
- New Ravens OC Declan Doyle "Taking Note", of rookie standout WR Ja'Kobi Lane after first week of Baltimore's OTAs. Not only has Lane provided immense energy/personality during OTAs, on the field is where he has really shown the most.
- Veteran NYG WR Darnell Mooney on his role with the Giants for the 2026 NFL Season: "my role is to make plays and dominate."
- BUF TE Dalton Kincaid heading into year four of his NFL career, says "This is the most confident I have felt both physically and mentally since entering the league". A recent history of fibula fractures for NFL skill players

We've seen them return quickly. But effectiveness falls off a cliff for up to a year after:

Tony Pollard

- Returned for OTAs after January 22nd injury (~4 months)

- Fell from 1.02 fantasy points/opportunity in 2022 to 0.70 in 2023 (on only 75 more touches)

Mark Andrews

- Returned for the AFC Championship game after November 16th injury (~2 months)

- Fell from 14.6 FPG pre-injury in 2023 to 11.1 FPG in 2024, with a major decrease in route participation

Isiah Pacheco

- Returned in Week 13 after Week 2 injury (~2.5 months)

- Fell from 0.84 fantasy points/opportunity in 2023 to 0.45 for the rest of 2024 + playoffs, and still just 0.60 in 2025

Chris Godwin

- Returned 11 months after Week 7 2024 injury (also the oldest player on this list when sustained, and had the most visibly gruesome dislocation on top of it)

- Fell from 19.7 FPG/the best season of his career in 2024, to just 9.2 FPG in 2025. YPRR fell from 2.62 to 1.51

Quinshon Judkins

- Expected back on the field in some fashion for training camp (starts in late July) after late December injury (~7 months)

Cam Skattebo
- Hopes to be cleared for training camp (starts in late July) after late October injury (~9 months)

I'd posit: should we even care about positive-sounding injury news for Skattebo or Judkins this offseason? 

The question was never whether they'd be on the field for Week 1; it's whether they'll be anything close to themselves!

Cam Skattebo is a tough price for me to get behind in round 4.

The obvious concern is how he plays coming off the gruesome ankle injury and tightrope surgery, which has not been kind to players the year following said surgery (‘24 Andrews, ‘23 Pollard, ‘23 Kupp). If he does struggle, we could see the non-elite workload he was getting last year again when both he and Tyrone Tracy were healthy (56% touch share in those, 63% last game before injury). I worry specifically about Skattebo with this explosiveness-zapping injury, as he was already not all that dynamic to begin with.

The Giants are also projected to be a below average offense (22nd in implied total avg), so it’s not like he has a great environment to insulate potential performance concerns. I think there’s just much better clicks both at cost and straight up after Skattebo at RB (and all positions for that matter).

yet, Cam Skattebo, who we haven't really seen running or doing anything full speed yet, is still going ~11-12 ADP spots ahead.

David Montgomery has become too rich for my blood at cost. It seems pretty clear he’s going to get a bunch of rushing volume, but I think the chances of Woody Marks having a significant role in the receiving game/in general are being undervalued. Marks is a poor runner, but he was a strong receiving prospect that could definitely take on that role, and was trusted heavily as a rookie by Houston.

This is also probably not going to be a great offense (Stroud uncertainty, outside top 20 EPA/Play both of L2 years), and Montgomery is aging (29 y/o) + showed some signs of decline last year (career low MTF and rush yards, lowest YACo/Att since 2021, lowest PFF grade in DET). 

He feels very dead-zoney at the 4/5 turn, a volume-based bet rather than fantasy friendly profile (questionable receiving role and explosive ability in a similarly questionable environment). Give me the higher upside/better environment RBs in the same range (Bucky, Swift, Tuten).

Really think folks are putting the cart before the horse with David Montgomery at an ADP of 49.5, and here is why:

- Folks are just "perceiving" this OL will be SO much better after the Texans spent 2 of their first 4 picks in the NFL draft on the positions. I am still very skeptical that it still may be below average.

-  Monty has just TWO seasons as he heads into his age-29 season with over 1k rushing yards. He held onto the starting lead role at CHI for 3 seasons before heading to DET where he backed up Gibbs. 

- 24, 38, 29 targets over his L3 seasons. Houston has an excellent receiving back in Woody Marks that was basically drafted FOR that reason. Feel he will see targets, but it is highly capped by Marks here.

- While NOT in Detroit with their OL, Monty has never topped 8 TDs (rushing), while only getting receiving TDs in his CHI years. (zero L3 seasons).

I feel like this is one of those "volume is king plays", but I think he is price ABOVE his ceiling currently. Don't shoot the messenger!

The Gainwell buzz + Bucky Irving injury fear is pushing him into a very comfortable range of drafts, behind Skatt and just ahead of TreVeyon/Montgomery.

Gainwell is a career backup that got spammed targets by a 40 y/o QB who can’t throw downfield anymore. He’s a fine player but by no means a difference maker,  should likely play a similar role to Rachaad White. And there’s a world where Sean Tucker takes goal line work again, but TB does have a new OC, minimal investment in him, AND that trend didn’t start until after Bucky’s 2-month injury absence.

Bucky is 1 year removed from an incredible rookie year (RB7 in back half of season, top of league efficiency), and dealt with injuries for the majority of the 2025 season. Prior to his 2025 injury, he still had an elite role too (RB9 in XFP/G per @FantasyPtsData).

Now going in the range of drafts where RBs all have a lot more uncertainty, I feel much better about clicking him in this late 4th range rather than late 3rd/early 4th. That uncertainty of Gainwell taking too much receiving or Tucker even taking GL work is much more palatable in this range. He’s going to be a pretty big target of mine if he remains a late 4th round (RB20) ADP or later, we've seen a much higher ceiling than that and the set-up is relatively similar.

Cam Skattebo

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

For dynasty purposes, my ranks go:

WR23 - Emeka Egbuka (WR11 ADP)
+ Another crack at clear WR1 role w/ no Evans, theoretical bounce-back from late 2025 woes in play or he and Baker after incredible start to his career. But, way too much overconfidence in Egbuka being a stud/bonafide WR1 at WR11 ADP for me, and that a bounce-back will occur for both he and Baker.

WR24 - KC Concepcion (WR30 ADP)
+ Really strong prospect with fantasy friendly skillset (YAC, separator) and clear path to strong volume, offense live to be much improved in 2027 w/ new QB. Downside is the offense doesn't improve or KCC doesn't emerge talent wise, but I feel good about his cost.

WR26 - Rome Odunze (WR20 ADP)
+ Top 10 pick who appeared to possibly be breaking out in year 2 before some injuries, still very live to be WR1 in one of the league's most exciting offenses. But I'm below ADP because of the similarly very real chance that he's the 3rd most prominent target in a run-heavier offense for multiple years, and he has not strung together a full season of impressive play for 2 straight years now.

Yup, the Gibbs/Henderson comps are pretty baseless except that they're both explosive players. To put some more context behind it:

Henderson was the RB24 in hppr FP/G as a rookie, while Gibbs was the RB11.

Henderson got off to a significantly slower start, with zero finishes above 12 hprr points in his 1st 10 games. Gibbs had 4 finishes above 18 FP by this time, and 3 of them above 23 FP.

Henderson also got completely iced in the Patriots playoff games (easily out-touched every game of playoffs, and Rhamondre had 37 of 49 touches in final 2 games), while Gibbs of course did not lose touches down the stretch.

Henderson was a 2nd round pick (#38 overall), while Gibbs was the 12th pick in the draft, just a different caliber of prospect.

Efficiency wise, the numbers ended up mostly *fine* for Hendo with some major outlier runs (not a bad thing), but were really poor for most of the season and nowhere near the range of Gibbs' incredible Y1. Henderson finished his rookie year with a 5.6% explosive rate, 0.13 MTF/att, and 1.91 YACo/Att, while Gibbs was 8.2% explosive, 0.26 MTF/Att, and 2.84 YACo/Att (all top of NFL marks).

Just assuming Henderson is going to follow the Gibbs path is generating a random comparison based on nothing really. These guys are not the same caliber of prospect or rookie year player.

Pretty healthy deal for Christian Watson but that's just what the 2026 market is.

It's becoming increasingly clear that GB views Watson as a legitimate WR1. He's been quite an efficient player in his 1st 4 years (2.4 career YPRR and above 2.5 in 3/4 seasons are both elite numbers). 

After playing more of a field-stretching role the prior 2 years (18+ aDOT), he showed a good bit more versatility in 2025 with a career high target share (18.2%), notably lower aDOT (15.3), and easily career highs in efficiency against zone coverage (2.87 YPRR), all while maintaining his production as a deep threat (highest % of his team's air yards and deep receiving YPG of his career).

He was working back from a major injury this year and still managed to post career-best efficiency/target-earning numbers. This contract shouldn't really change much for 2026 (already poised for probably the biggest role of his career), and I'll probably near full-fade if he rises in ADP this summer (just given other weapons I like in this offense at cheaper costs in Kraft/Reed), but this feels bullish for his long-term/dynasty outlook.

Ashton Jeanty remains my favorite click at the 1-2 turn:

+ Goes from the stone worst offense in the NFL to the Kubiak system that completely elevated the Seattle offense

+ Incredible prospect (#1 all time in my model that dates back to 2017)

+ Extremely minimal talent behind him so “committee” talks seem overblown, pattern matching usage between different offenses/players (SEA last year) typically does not work out 

+ When given space last year (which happened rarely), Jeanty’s numbers looked a lot better. He led the NFL in YACo/Att and was 6th best in stuffed run rate when given 1+ yards before contact.

+ Elite receiving volume as a rookie, 1 of 7 RBs above 70 targets

+ TD equity with the improved system, OL, and QB play in LV should be much higher

I just can’t find anything not to like about Jeanty at this price, RB1 overall seems firmly in his range of outcomes.

Those folks know how QB contests work, whether fully agreeing or not. 

Most recently signed QB temporarily ranking *fifth* is not a big deal.

Heavily agree with the sentiment here, the entire QB2 to QB18 range clustering in ADP has made some of the elites a lot more enticing (has led me to heavy Maye and JD5 exposure).

I don’t have the numbers for past year ADP on UD but feel like the ~QB8-12 range is also quite boosted up from years past (mid round 7 to R8). Drafting those guys so close to QB2-5 feels pretty gross, when you can get much higher upside bets a round or two earlier and very similarly projecting QBs beyond round 8 (Purdy, Goff, Love, Nix, etc).

QB is obviously team dependent and you’ll have guys you like in various ranges, but I’ve made it a point to avoid most of that QB8-12 range unless they fall.

David Montgomery has become too rich for my blood at cost. It seems pretty clear he’s going to get a bunch of rushing volume, but I think the chances of Woody Marks having a significant role in the receiving game/in general are being undervalued. Marks is a poor runner, but he was a strong receiving prospect that could definitely take on that role, and was trusted heavily as a rookie by Houston.

This is also probably not going to be a great offense (Stroud uncertainty, outside top 20 EPA/Play both of L2 years), and Montgomery is aging (29 y/o) + showed some signs of decline last year (career low MTF and rush yards, lowest YACo/Att since 2021, lowest PFF grade in DET). 

He feels very dead-zoney at the 4/5 turn, a volume-based bet rather than fantasy friendly profile (questionable receiving role and explosive ability in a similarly questionable environment). Give me the higher upside/better environment RBs in the same range (Bucky, Swift, Tuten).

7 early Fantasy Football takes/questions after firing off some best ball drafts:

- Who is taking Chuba Hubbard in the 6th round?!
Related: Jonathan Brooks looks like an awesome value in the 100's.

- 13 QBs going in the top-100 with only one going in the top-50 (Josh Allen, 30th) is unique!

- Luther Burden as WR21/#43 overall is going to look ridiculous one way or another. Either he was way too expensive after a small sample late-season breakout or far too cheap after balling out in a limited role and set for a bigger role.

- MHJ went 4th overall in 2024 and was being drafted in the second round as WR9 in fantasy. Carnell Tate went 4th, and Jordyn Tyson went 8th, and their current ADP's are WR30/31 and pick 61/62 overall.

- Are we sure the Jaguars even like Bhayshul Tuten? 4th round pick, wasn't all that good as a rookie, signed CRod, rumored to have wanted to trade up in NFL Draft for a RB. Feels like some fantasy-wishcasting taking him at the 5/6 turn.

- Exposures to Parker Washington vs BTJ could be a sizable decision point.

- Last year Matthew Golden got pushed up into the 70's of ADP as a rookie. After coming on late in the season and both Wicks + Doubs moving on, and the Packers still seemingly reluctant to play Reed in non-11 personnel, Golden's ADP is 109. I'm buying the dip

So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀

- Denver HC Sean Payton was asked "Who is the WR1 between veteran Courtland Sutton and newcomer Jaylen Waddle?", his response: "We’re not just going to have. So, we don’t have [that]. We just play different packages and try to give those guys the things that they do well.”
- The Houston Texans and star WR Nico Collins agreed on a contract negotiation/adjustment that guarantees Collins' salary for the next two NFL seasons, while also getting a pay increase of $8m for this season and $9m for next season.

On Bhayshul Tuten:

“The Jaguars did sign veteran free agent Chris Rodriguez Jr. who will do his own fair share of replacing Etienne's touches in the Jaguars' running game, but Tuten has more of a three-down skill-set that is closer to what Etienne did a year ago.”

I’m a big Tuten bull but can’t get on board with Dobbins not meaning anything if he had signed with JAX. Him and C-Rod are not the same caliber RB, so I think Tuten’s outlook could’ve been much different/worse with Dobbins there.

That being said, they of course didn’t sign Dobbins. And that makes me feel even better about Tuten now. C-Rod was seemingly less of a priority than many are making him out to be (I don’t think Coen coaching him in college 5 years ago proves anything at all), and he’s nowhere near the pedigree of Dobbins. His signing, IMO, makes the Tuten breakout much more likely than it would’ve been w/ Dobbins.

I don’t think JAX’s plans for Tuten have  to be as binary as they’re being perceived, in that their plans w/ Tuten alongside Dobbins could’ve been much different than their current plans for him.

David Montgomery has become too rich for my blood at cost. It seems pretty clear he’s going to get a bunch of rushing volume, but I think the chances of Woody Marks having a significant role in the receiving game/in general are being undervalued. Marks is a poor runner, but he was a strong receiving prospect that could definitely take on that role, and was trusted heavily as a rookie by Houston.

This is also probably not going to be a great offense (Stroud uncertainty, outside top 20 EPA/Play both of L2 years), and Montgomery is aging (29 y/o) + showed some signs of decline last year (career low MTF and rush yards, lowest YACo/Att since 2021, lowest PFF grade in DET). 

He feels very dead-zoney at the 4/5 turn, a volume-based bet rather than fantasy friendly profile (questionable receiving role and explosive ability in a similarly questionable environment). Give me the higher upside/better environment RBs in the same range (Bucky, Swift, Tuten).

- Rookie CLE WR Denzel Boston makes "play of the day" on a long go-ball from veteran Deshaun Watson resulting in an over-the-shoulder TD grab in Thursday's OTAs.
- Newly acquired BUF WR DJ Moore on his 2026 season outlook: "I am ready to show everybody that I can still do what I do and do it with the Bills." 👀
- 2nd-Year New Orleans QB Tyler Shough on recently 8th overall pick WR Jordyn Tyson: "He's an ELITE separator, going to be VERY friendly to the quarterback!"
- Reports swirling around Buccaneers camp is that year five for HC Todd Bowles is "make or break" in Tampa.
- Chief's GM Brett Veach on QB Patrick Mahomes recovery from ACL/LCL knee injury: "Needless to say, he is well ahead of schedule, but we know the type of competitor Mahomes is and the biggest challenge is protecting him from himself (in terms or moving too fast/doing too much in OTAs)."
- 10-year NYG Veteran DL Roy Robertson-Harris tears Achilles in Thursday's OTAs; will now miss all of the 2026 NFL season. Unfortunate break, as now two DL for an already thin Giants defense are sidelined for this season.
- Jags Beat @_John_Shipley on mailbag question "is CRod the Jag's RB1 over Tuten?": "Think it will be a committee approach and no true RB1. More of a "hot hand" here and there and will use multiple RBs to set the tone. Do think (CRod) gets more carries than Tuten, but not by much."
- New LV Raiders HC Klint Kubiak mirrors same sentiment as 2nd-year Stud RB Ashton Jeanty: "If I don't have to, I don't want to come off the field." Kubiak went on to say, "team wants to put a lot of pressure on [Jeanty]." 🚀
- Rumor Mill: "Rumors surfacing that the KC Chiefs were inquired from the Eagles about a possible trade for A.J. Brown, Chiefs said, "No"." Brown also allegedly gave a list of teams he would prefer to play for including: Patriots, Chiefs, Bills, & Chargers.
- 4th-Year GB TE Tucker Kraft is progressing well in his recovery from a torn ACL suffered in Week 9 of the 2025 NFL season, videos capturing Kraft sprinting and adding resistance for strength training, signs are very positive so far! 👍
- New Eagles OC Sean Mannion on Rookie WR Makai Lemon: “Makai is very impressive, he’s a tremendous young talent, takes a lot of pride in the little detail. With his degree of talent and potential the sky is the limit".
- TEN 2nd-Year QB Cameron Ward has shed 10 pounds during the offseason but watching his calories and upping his cardio regime. Dare we say, "Best shape of his life" SZN is HERE? 😜
- Commanders HC Dan Quinn on rookie WR Antonio Williams: "Has absolutely jumped out. As a route runner, he can stop, he can change directions, explosives, this guy’s got real natural feel as a receiver. Antonio has it, man, in the biggest way.” 🔥
- New Ravens OC Declan Doyle "Taking Note", of rookie standout WR Ja'Kobi Lane after first week of Baltimore's OTAs. Not only has Lane provided immense energy/personality during OTAs, on the field is where he has really shown the most.
- Veteran NYG WR Darnell Mooney on his role with the Giants for the 2026 NFL Season: "my role is to make plays and dominate."
- BUF TE Dalton Kincaid heading into year four of his NFL career, says "This is the most confident I have felt both physically and mentally since entering the league". Kincaid has dealt with multiple injuries over the last two seasons including knee and PCL issues, but all signs point to positive outlook for 2026.

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/26:

- As expected, 3rd-year TB RB Bucky Irving is NOT present on the field at OTAs as he is still recovering and rehabbing on injured shoulder that required surgery. HC Todd Bowles when asked about Irvings return: "Sometime in the summer or fall". 😕
- TEN QB Cam Ward on 1st-Rd Rookie Carnell Tate after first week of OTAs: "He'll have an explosive year just because he'll get a lot of one-on-one matchups. That's what we want. We want guys to come down and play, man, because we think we can beat him." 🚀
- 3rd-Year HOU TE Cade Stover has begun taking reps at FB while also serving as TE depth for the Texans. The Ohio State product has yet to play a full season due to injuries and is looking for a way to remain on the roster after cut-down time competing with the likes of Brevin Jordan, Foster Moreau, and Harrison Bryant.
- RUMOR MILL: Could Veteran SF WR Brandon Aiyuk be a low-risk, high-reward match to the New York Giants, who could possibly be without Malik Nabers to begin the 2026 season?
-  NY Jets want to give rookie QB Cade Klubnik a "good look and evaluation" before making any final decisions on signing a back-up veteran QB behind starter Geno Smith.
- Bear's QB Coach J.T. Barrett on emphasis for year three for starting QB Caleb Williams: "More efficiency, Less Superhero Plays". 
- Pelissero expects "fair competition going in" for the Minnesota QBs Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy heading into training camp. “If this were just, it’s Kyler’s job, and J.J. is competing with Carson Wentz to be the two, you would have traded J.J."
- Per @AlbertBreer: Chiefs, Chargers, Bills and Rams all had their shot at this already at AJ Brown and are all likely out." Stating the obvious also, what is most likely scenario is a reunion with Mike Vrabel.
- Raider's HC Klint Kubiak reiterates once again after second day of OTAs pertaining to 2nd year stud RB Ashton Jeanty on a potential snap split in the backfield: "I don't know the play snap percentage, but you look at Christian McCaffrey, his play snap percentage is high. So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀
- CAR RB Jonathon Brooks participating and looks to be a full go in early OTAs, reports are that he has "bulked up" a little and is already flashing nicely on run and passing game.
- Denver HC Sean Payton was asked "Who is the WR1 between veteran Courtland Sutton and newcomer Jaylen Waddle?", his response: "We’re not just going to have. So, we don’t have [that]. We just play different packages and try to give those guys the things that they do well.”
- The Houston Texans and star WR Nico Collins agreed on a contract negotiation/adjustment that guarantees Collins' salary for the next two NFL seasons, while also getting a pay increase of $8m for this season and $9m for next season. 
- 2nd-Year Jag's WR/CB Travis Hunter is indeed participating in Jaguar's OTAs this week. Was seen doing something quite unspeakable today - catching a pass on a WR route from QB Trevor Lawrence. We thought he only played CB now.
- Jacksonville HC Liam Coen was asked about why newly signed RB Chris Rodriguez Jr. was NOT in attendance for first day of OTAs: "Yeah, you know I mean, the injury report will come out at some point." 
- Per @Schultz_Report, Arizona Cardinals have fielded calls from teams around the league pertaining to trade discussions centered around Pro-Bowl DE/EDGE Josh Sweat.
- 3rd-year TB WR Jalen McMillan on excitement of opening the playbook under new OC Zac Robinson: "We had a lot of plays last year [under Josh Grizzard] that weren't called, but right now we are repping a lot of them and giving the coaches confidence in calling them."
- Jaguar's OC Grant Udinksi gushes over WR Parker Washington in Post-OTA interview: "I think really, really highly of Parker. I hope Parker thinks really, really highly of himself, because we think that he is capable of what he did at the end of the season last year and even more than that." 🚀

Milwaukee Brewers' starting pitcher Kyle Harrison posted his 3rd game with 10 or more strikeouts in 2026 while facing the Giants on Tuesday. Harrison was cooking with an 18% swinging-strike rate and a 34% ball rate on Tuesday. 

⏩Harrison pounded the zone with his four-seam (60%) while eliciting a 19.4% swinging-strike rate on Tuesday. For context, Harrison was throwing his four-seam in the zone 56.6% of the time with a 15.6% swinging-strike rate. The four-seam whiffs align with Harrison, who added nearly three inches of induced vertical break.
🪝The slurve induced an 18.2% swinging-strike rate on Tuesday, though he typically located them low and below the zone. Harrison's slurve has been an effective pitch to right-handed hitters (.103 wOBA, .183 xwOBA) and lefties (.187 wOBA, .247 xwOBA). 

Harrison has typically relied on his high-end command, but we've seen his stuff improve recently. It's hard to decide whether to buy high.

Milwaukee Brewers' starting pitcher Kyle Harrison threw five scoreless innings against the Padres, allowing five hits, zero walks, and seven strikeouts. Harrison had a 17.2% swinging-strike rate and a 34% ball rate on Thursday.

👉The Padres stacked right-handed hitters (88%) against Harrison. He consistently threw four-seamers, slurves, and changeups against righties on Thursday. Harrison had the four-seamer cooking by generating a 21% swinging-strike rate. The four-seamer added 1 mph of velocity on Thursday, with very little fluctuation in the movement profile, as a heater he throws in the upper parts of the zone. 
🪫Harrison located his slurve and changeup low in the zone to pair well with his four-seamers up in the zone. Interestingly, Harrison's changeup (+4) and slurve (+2) added 2 or more inches of downward movement, meaning they could be pitches to elicit whiffs. He has three pitches with a double-digit swinging-strike rate in 2026, including his four-seam (16.5%), curveball (11.5%), and changeup (10.7%).

Harrison has shown high-end command (112 Location+) and above-average stuff, with an overall 112 Stuff+.

You are going to want to hear what Liam Coen said about Parker Washington today

Everything the #Jaguars have said about Parker Washington since Liam Coen became head coach — pulled from the Coachspeak Index Discord, and presented by @DraftKings:

HC LIAM COEN (07/25/25): Parker Washington has had a really good start, I’ve been pleased with him. He’s gone up and made plays. He’s got the right mindset, he’s strong, he can kind of work second-level releases on people when they try to get hands on him.

OC GRANT UDINSKI (08/06/25): Parker Washington can win consistently against man coverage and tight coverage. He can make plays on the ball and attack the ball with his hands.

We can trust him at different spots.

COEN (08/17/25): Parker Washington just consistently keeps competing at a high level. Every day, he makes a play. Every day, he’s in the right spot at the right time, working his tail off. I love his play energy. He doesn’t really fatigue that much, he’s in great physical shape. I’ve been very pleased with Parker.

UDINSKI (08/20/25): It’s a huge range of route tree, blocking assignments, and different alignments that Parker Washington has to manage, so just the mental aspect of him being able to gain the level of mastery to go out there and play one position on one play, and then turn around the very next play and play a totally different position in a different spot and be asked to express a different skill set on a play-to-play basis is really impressive. And then you see the plays that he makes when the ball is thrown his way.

You saw the flashes of him being able to play all three positions, it was just ultimately about building that level of consistency and giving him the opportunities to go out there and make those plays, which he’s taken advantage of.

“I certainly think he’ll be a really big part of our offense. Like I said, the plays that he’s making, that flexibility he provides to play multiple positions, sets him up for that success.”

COEN (09/17/25): [on whether Parker Washington has earned a bigger role in the offense] Absolutely. He has continued to ascend and perform on the practice field, in the meeting rooms, and on Sundays. When we went into last week, we wanted to get him a bigger role and to get him going. We didn’t do a good enough job of that in Week 1. He’s absolutely earned a bigger role.

UDINSKI (09/18/25): Parker Washington’s shiftiness poses a challenge for defenders. The other great thing he’s done is being able to track the ball and get it.

COEN (09/21/25): We were kinda going into this game with a little bit more for Parker Washington, based off of last week and the way that he practiced all week.

COEN (09/22/25): Parker Washington had a phenomenal week of practice last week. I have lost zero confidence in him.

COEN (09/24/25): [on Parker Washington’s drops] I have not lost any confidence in Parker, by any means. Stuff happens. He works his tail off every single day. Last week was his best week of practice since we’ve been here.

UDINSKI (09/25/25): Parker Washington has made plays when he’s had opportunities. I think the way he’s worked and continued to progress, he’ll continue to have opportunities to make plays.

COEN (11/09/25): Parker Washington keeps making plays. We were trying to get him the rock multiple ways. He just keeps doing his thing, he’s helping us win every week, and I appreciate him for that.

COEN (11/26/25): Parker Washington has been one of our top performing receivers since training camp. He’s worked his tail off to learn the system since we got here, he’s one of the first guys in the building every day, takes super care of his body, very intentional about the way he practices and goes about his business. He’s made plays for us in a lot of ways on Sundays. He’s definitely trusted, he’s somebody we count on to continue to make plays.

COEN (12/21/25): Parker Washington is so explosive. He’s so strong, his lower half is very explosive. He works extremely hard to prepare in the offseason and during the season.

COEN (12/28/25): Parker Washington is so reliable as a competitor. You know where he’s gonna be, you know what he’s gonna do. Trevor Lawrence trusts him, there’s a trust level there, there’s a continuity there. He has a good football feel. He’s been able to make people miss and have a lot of run-after-catch, which really helps your offense.

COEN (01/11/26): Parker Washington had a great season, especially towards the tail end. He just continued to rise to the occasion. He’s such a tough competitor, he played his ass off. I appreciate Parker to the fullest, and look forward to working with him in the future.

GM JAMES GLADSTONE (01/14/26): Sometimes you question whether what you’re seeing in training camp is a real reflection of what you’ll see in the regular season. With Parker Washington, training camp didn’t lie to us. That was no fraudulent exposure, that was an accurate interpretation. When his opp presented itself, he certainly stepped up and helped our cause.

COEN (05/26/26): [on what the next step is for Parker Washington] He’s done a nice job so far this spring. I thought we saw some flashes early last season, and there was consistency towards the end of the season when he was a dominating presence with the things he was able to do in the slot and also outside for us. I think activating him a little bit, continuing to work on the ball-in-hand stuff that he can do at a high level because of the way he’s built and wired. Continuing to work on the screen game and those things, getting him the ball on jet sweeps and some of those things. And then the complete route tree of slot and outside and doing all those things is a step he can take.

UDINSKI (05/26/26): [on expectations for Parker Washington] I think really, really highly of Parker. We think that he’s capable of what he did at the end of last season, and more than that. Our expectation for him is to continue to grow and do even more than he did last year. He’s a highly, highly capable player, who’s capable of even more.

COEN (06/09/26): [on what kind of trust Parker Washington has earned with Coen over the last year] A ton. He communicates what he sees on the field. The relationship between he and Trevor Lawrence has continued to grow and develop. His toolbox allows him to play inside, play outside, run routes at all three levels. I think his football knowledge has just continued to ascend. I just think his overall knowledge has really elevated over the last year, so that makes it a lot easier to work with somebody and to trust them that they’re gonna do the right thing on gameday.

My honest-to-goodness evaluation of Caleb Williams:

He improved dramatically YoY and made the playoffs last year. But objectively, he hasn't played the position at a high level. I'd say he's been well below-average in both years.

He can make insane "wow" throws only a few other QBs can make. That's really cool! But it's also massively overrated. These are rare events that matter significantly less than being a consistently accurate passer who avoids negative plays. Highlight reel throws stick in the back of the mind of the average fan, who weights them too heavily as if one sick throw is the same as making 99/100 easy completions or cancels out 3 turnover-worthy throws plus 6 sacks. It doesn't. Not even close.

From a real NFL standpoint, I do think he's overrated and that his PR has been much better than he's deserved. But I still really like him from a fantasy perspective. Chicago's supporting cast (including Ben Johnson calling the plays) almost feels like an impossible-to-fail setup, or even an impossible-not-to-be-great situation.

Caleb Williams finished as the QB8 last year (19.2 FPG) despite ranking bottom-5 in every QB accuracy stat imaginable. What happens if he's closer to average this year? Or what if he's actually really good?

Ben Johnson on Caleb Williams:

"Asking Caleb to continue to grow within the offense - times where the primary receiver is open and we want him to deliver him an accurate football within the timing of the play and then progress if the protection allows us"

Trevor Lawrence made some wildly impressive throws in 2025. We always knew the arm talent was top-notch, but it didn’t all come together until paired up with Liam Coen.

Coen empowered him to:

- use his legs more as a playmaker
- trust his reads (pre-snap motion to give intel on defensive looks + route concepts designed to confirm/attack post-snap)
- unlock all areas of the field (career-high middle-field throw rate in 2025)

Pretty healthy deal for Christian Watson but that's just what the 2026 market is.

It's becoming increasingly clear that GB views Watson as a legitimate WR1. He's been quite an efficient player in his 1st 4 years (2.4 career YPRR and above 2.5 in 3/4 seasons are both elite numbers). 

After playing more of a field-stretching role the prior 2 years (18+ aDOT), he showed a good bit more versatility in 2025 with a career high target share (18.2%), notably lower aDOT (15.3), and easily career highs in efficiency against zone coverage (2.87 YPRR), all while maintaining his production as a deep threat (highest % of his team's air yards and deep receiving YPG of his career).

He was working back from a major injury this year and still managed to post career-best efficiency/target-earning numbers. This contract shouldn't really change much for 2026 (already poised for probably the biggest role of his career), and I'll probably near full-fade if he rises in ADP this summer (just given other weapons I like in this offense at cheaper costs in Kraft/Reed), but this feels bullish for his long-term/dynasty outlook.

Been grabbing a ton of Tucker Kraft in drafts. He showcased his top-of-position upside in the 2025 season, yet is priced only a couple rounds more expensive than last year.

Certainly not a doctor, but I feel like the way Kraft wins/scores most of his FP won’t be all that affected by him coming off the ACL injury. He doesn’t really rely on top-end speed much or quick twitch movements as a YAC threat and route runner, but rather his explosiveness and strength with the ball in his hands. From what I understand, this will be less affected than the speed/quickness aspect if he’s limited by the ACL recovery.

Kraft also has less target competition in 2026 in what should be a similarly strong offense, making for some appealing TD equity again. I suspect the environment could give him some stability/insulation for a solid year even if he’s not himself, and we’ve already seen the upside he has if he’s near full strength. I think he’s a very strong risk/reward bet at the 7/8 turn.

I think there’s a good bit of context missing to this Waddle/Sutton comparison.

The Dolphins offense and Tua have been absolutely awful the last two years. In 2024, the OL was so bad they had to just throw short and quick all game (5.9! aDOT), a role being taken by 3 other players (Jonnu, Tyreek, Achane). In 2025, Tua was just so bad and the Dolphins offense was a disaster which hurt production, yet Waddle’s efficiency was still really strong.

Waddle has been playing a ton of X these past couple years, a role both not most beneficial to his skillset and to the remaining ability Tua had. And he’s still remained more efficient/the better target-earner than Sutton despite this (0.26 vs 0.22 TPRR last year). Kick this comparison back one more year (last time MIA offense was competent) and the numbers look drastically different.

Courtland Sutton has been facing zero target comp the last 2 years in a much better offense, and he still managed to fade out for parts of 2025. He’s a fine player but has never really been a target-earner (0.22 TPRR since ‘21) despite mostly poor tgt comp.

I’m of the opinion that Waddle will thrive in the Z/slot role in Denver, a role more conducive to his skillset and in a much better environment. I think the Sutton price is fine enough, but Waddle has demonstrated much higher upside (elite efficiency, multiple 15+ hppr FP/G seasons). I’m betting he realizes that upside in a much improved environment

A recent history of fibula fractures for NFL skill players

We've seen them return quickly. But effectiveness falls off a cliff for up to a year after:

Tony Pollard

- Returned for OTAs after January 22nd injury (~4 months)

- Fell from 1.02 fantasy points/opportunity in 2022 to 0.70 in 2023 (on only 75 more touches)

Mark Andrews

- Returned for the AFC Championship game after November 16th injury (~2 months)

- Fell from 14.6 FPG pre-injury in 2023 to 11.1 FPG in 2024, with a major decrease in route participation

Isiah Pacheco

- Returned in Week 13 after Week 2 injury (~2.5 months)

- Fell from 0.84 fantasy points/opportunity in 2023 to 0.45 for the rest of 2024 + playoffs, and still just 0.60 in 2025

Chris Godwin

- Returned 11 months after Week 7 2024 injury (also the oldest player on this list when sustained, and had the most visibly gruesome dislocation on top of it)

A recent history of fibula fractures for NFL skill players

We've seen them return quickly. But effectiveness falls off a cliff for up to a year after:

Tony Pollard

- Returned for OTAs after January 22nd injury (~4 months)

- Fell from 1.02 fantasy points/opportunity in 2022 to 0.70 in 2023 (on only 75 more touches)

Mark Andrews

- Returned for the AFC Championship game after November 16th injury (~2 months)

- Fell from 14.6 FPG pre-injury in 2023 to 11.1 FPG in 2024, with a major decrease in route participation

Isiah Pacheco

- Returned in Week 13 after Week 2 injury (~2.5 months)

- Fell from 0.84 fantasy points/opportunity in 2023 to 0.45 for the rest of 2024 + playoffs, and still just 0.60 in 2025

Chris Godwin

- Returned 11 months after Week 7 2024 injury (also the oldest player on this list when sustained, and had the most visibly gruesome dislocation on top of it)

- Fell from 19.7 FPG/the best season of his career in 2024, to just 9.2 FPG in 2025. YPRR fell from 2.62 to 1.51

Quinshon Judkins

- Expected back on the field in some fashion for training camp (starts in late July) after late December injury (~7 months)

Cam Skattebo
- Hopes to be cleared for training camp (starts in late July) after late October injury (~9 months)

Cam Skattebo is a tough price for me to get behind in round 4.

The obvious concern is how he plays coming off the gruesome ankle injury and tightrope surgery, which has not been kind to players the year following said surgery (‘24 Andrews, ‘23 Pollard, ‘23 Kupp). If he does struggle, we could see the non-elite workload he was getting last year again when both he and Tyrone Tracy were healthy (56% touch share in those, 63% last game before injury). I worry specifically about Skattebo with this explosiveness-zapping injury, as he was already not all that dynamic to begin with.

The Giants are also projected to be a below average offense (22nd in implied total avg), so it’s not like he has a great environment to insulate potential performance concerns. I think there’s just much better clicks both at cost and straight up after Skattebo at RB (and all positions for that matter).
Tight rope for Khalil Shakir - there’s no one size fits all but we’ve seen players typically struggle to start the season (Pollard, Andrews, & Kupp come to mind)

Pretty healthy deal for Christian Watson but that's just what the 2026 market is.

It's becoming increasingly clear that GB views Watson as a legitimate WR1. He's been quite an efficient player in his 1st 4 years (2.4 career YPRR and above 2.5 in 3/4 seasons are both elite numbers). 

After playing more of a field-stretching role the prior 2 years (18+ aDOT), he showed a good bit more versatility in 2025 with a career high target share (18.2%), notably lower aDOT (15.3), and easily career highs in efficiency against zone coverage (2.87 YPRR), all while maintaining his production as a deep threat (highest % of his team's air yards and deep receiving YPG of his career).

He was working back from a major injury this year and still managed to post career-best efficiency/target-earning numbers. This contract shouldn't really change much for 2026 (already poised for probably the biggest role of his career), and I'll probably near full-fade if he rises in ADP this summer (just given other weapons I like in this offense at cheaper costs in Kraft/Reed), but this feels bullish for his long-term/dynasty outlook.

Everything the #Jaguars have said about Parker Washington since Liam Coen became head coach — pulled from the Coachspeak Index Discord, and presented by @DraftKings:

HC LIAM COEN (07/25/25): Parker Washington has had a really good start, I’ve been pleased with him. He’s gone up and made plays. He’s got the right mindset, he’s strong, he can kind of work second-level releases on people when they try to get hands on him.

OC GRANT UDINSKI (08/06/25): Parker Washington can win consistently against man coverage and tight coverage. He can make plays on the ball and attack the ball with his hands.

We can trust him at different spots.

COEN (08/17/25): Parker Washington just consistently keeps competing at a high level. Every day, he makes a play. Every day, he’s in the right spot at the right time, working his tail off. I love his play energy. He doesn’t really fatigue that much, he’s in great physical shape. I’ve been very pleased with Parker.

UDINSKI (08/20/25): It’s a huge range of route tree, blocking assignments, and different alignments that Parker Washington has to manage, so just the mental aspect of him being able to gain the level of mastery to go out there and play one position on one play, and then turn around the very next play and play a totally different position in a different spot and be asked to express a different skill set on a play-to-play basis is really impressive. And then you see the plays that he makes when the ball is thrown his way.

You saw the flashes of him being able to play all three positions, it was just ultimately about building that level of consistency and giving him the opportunities to go out there and make those plays, which he’s taken advantage of.

“I certainly think he’ll be a really big part of our offense. Like I said, the plays that he’s making, that flexibility he provides to play multiple positions, sets him up for that success.”

COEN (09/17/25): [on whether Parker Washington has earned a bigger role in the offense] Absolutely. He has continued to ascend and perform on the practice field, in the meeting rooms, and on Sundays. When we went into last week, we wanted to get him a bigger role and to get him going. We didn’t do a good enough job of that in Week 1. He’s absolutely earned a bigger role.

UDINSKI (09/18/25): Parker Washington’s shiftiness poses a challenge for defenders. The other great thing he’s done is being able to track the ball and get it.

COEN (09/21/25): We were kinda going into this game with a little bit more for Parker Washington, based off of last week and the way that he practiced all week.

COEN (09/22/25): Parker Washington had a phenomenal week of practice last week. I have lost zero confidence in him.

COEN (09/24/25): [on Parker Washington’s drops] I have not lost any confidence in Parker, by any means. Stuff happens. He works his tail off every single day. Last week was his best week of practice since we’ve been here.

UDINSKI (09/25/25): Parker Washington has made plays when he’s had opportunities. I think the way he’s worked and continued to progress, he’ll continue to have opportunities to make plays.

COEN (11/09/25): Parker Washington keeps making plays. We were trying to get him the rock multiple ways. He just keeps doing his thing, he’s helping us win every week, and I appreciate him for that.

COEN (11/26/25): Parker Washington has been one of our top performing receivers since training camp. He’s worked his tail off to learn the system since we got here, he’s one of the first guys in the building every day, takes super care of his body, very intentional about the way he practices and goes about his business. He’s made plays for us in a lot of ways on Sundays. He’s definitely trusted, he’s somebody we count on to continue to make plays.

COEN (12/21/25): Parker Washington is so explosive. He’s so strong, his lower half is very explosive. He works extremely hard to prepare in the offseason and during the season.

COEN (12/28/25): Parker Washington is so reliable as a competitor. You know where he’s gonna be, you know what he’s gonna do. Trevor Lawrence trusts him, there’s a trust level there, there’s a continuity there. He has a good football feel. He’s been able to make people miss and have a lot of run-after-catch, which really helps your offense.

COEN (01/11/26): Parker Washington had a great season, especially towards the tail end. He just continued to rise to the occasion. He’s such a tough competitor, he played his ass off. I appreciate Parker to the fullest, and look forward to working with him in the future.

GM JAMES GLADSTONE (01/14/26): Sometimes you question whether what you’re seeing in training camp is a real reflection of what you’ll see in the regular season. With Parker Washington, training camp didn’t lie to us. That was no fraudulent exposure, that was an accurate interpretation. When his opp presented itself, he certainly stepped up and helped our cause.

COEN (05/26/26): [on what the next step is for Parker Washington] He’s done a nice job so far this spring. I thought we saw some flashes early last season, and there was consistency towards the end of the season when he was a dominating presence with the things he was able to do in the slot and also outside for us. I think activating him a little bit, continuing to work on the ball-in-hand stuff that he can do at a high level because of the way he’s built and wired. Continuing to work on the screen game and those things, getting him the ball on jet sweeps and some of those things. And then the complete route tree of slot and outside and doing all those things is a step he can take.

UDINSKI (05/26/26): [on expectations for Parker Washington] I think really, really highly of Parker. We think that he’s capable of what he did at the end of last season, and more than that. Our expectation for him is to continue to grow and do even more than he did last year. He’s a highly, highly capable player, who’s capable of even more.

COEN (06/09/26): [on what kind of trust Parker Washington has earned with Coen over the last year] A ton. He communicates what he sees on the field. The relationship between he and Trevor Lawrence has continued to grow and develop. His toolbox allows him to play inside, play outside, run routes at all three levels. I think his football knowledge has just continued to ascend. I just think his overall knowledge has really elevated over the last year, so that makes it a lot easier to work with somebody and to trust them that they’re gonna do the right thing on gameday.

“The fact the Jaguars and Lawrence have made such a focus to push the ball downfield and he is still not making mistakes says it all.”

Kyle Pitts 2025 season + 2026 outlook feels like it’s going a bit under the radar, even if his ‘25 production was largely without Drake London on the field.

After a couple of down and injury-ridden seasons, Pitts showed us that he still has juice. He posted career highs in receptions, targets, and TDs, while posting his best TPRR (0.23) and YPRR (1.84) since 2022. The Falcons lined him up inline at a career high rate, allowing him to be a mismatch there rather than pretending he's a real WR. He might not be the guy we were expecting out of college, but he showed legitimate upside last year.

He remains the clear 2nd target in this passing game most likely, with the Falcons only adding 3rd RD rookie Zachariah Branch (and losing Mooney), so another 110+ target season seems more than possible. Most of his 2025 production did come with Drake London out or after his injury, but Pitts did have some decent production even before London started dealing with injury (3 of 6 games above 55 yards, north of 6 target per game).

There's fair concern that he disappears some or becomes a non-factor w/ London on the field, but the price outside the top 100 picks in late round 9 feels plenty cheap enough to find out given what he showed last year. And it's similarly fair to wonder if given what he showed later last year, that this new staff makes it more of a priority to get him the ball even with London active.

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

Feel like the 3QB trend on Underdog has gone way too far.

If you’re drafting 2 QBs inside the top 8 or 9 rounds, there’s typically no reason to draft a 3rd QB (unless it’s two round ~9 guys like Goff and Kyler, even then it’s up in the air for me). 

Just way too much capital spent to automatically take an extra dead roster spot per week at the flattest scoring position, with 2 stronger QBs already on your roster.

You’re just naturally limiting your upside when you allocate too much capital to QB, taking that extra dead roster spot weekly when you don’t need it (2 QBs that more than give you enough weekly scoring) and not giving enough capital to the other positions on your roster. The law of diminishing returns comes into play.

- Packers 3x Pro-Bowl, 1x All-Pro RB Josh Jacobs was arrested and booked into the Brown County jail upon allegations of FIVE charges (2 potential felonies) including battery/domestic abuse, criminal damage to property, disorderly conduct, strangulation & suffocation, and intimidation of a victim. - Great News out of KC as QB Patrick Mahomes was on the field working out in some capacity for the first day of the Chief's OTAs. 
- The 49ers and highly sought back-up QB Mac Jones have agreed on a revised contract that will add roughly $300,000 roster bonus to Jones' currently compensation. Jones is now set to make $3.55m base with another $2.25m up for grabs in incentives.
- Jaguar's HC Liam Coen on his RB Room: "It's a group that has a lot of opportunities open to them. There's a wide-open room. It's truly wide open."👀
- Journeyman ARI QB Jacoby Brissett continues his hold-out by still being non-present in Cardinal's on-going OTAs. Brissett is still looking for a revised/raised contract to be the team's starting QB.
- New Dolphin's HC Jeff Halsey says that RB DeVon Achane is still recovering from shoulder "clean up" surgery from injury sustained at the end of last season and isn't participating (fully) in OTAs as a result.
- 1st Day of OTAs in Minnesota, looks like 2nd-year Vikings QB J.J. McCarthy has cleaned up his footwork/throwing mechanics' while seemingly taking 1st team reps per Beat.
- KC Chief's WR Xavier Worthy "not ahead of schedule" pertaining to his recovery from a torn labrum in the early part of last season. Seen wearing a yellow "non-contact/injured" jersey in day 1 of OTAs.
- Packers HC Matt LaFleur on TE Tucker Kraft as he rehabs from ACL surgery recovery: “He looks so good, you’d want to put him out there, hopefully he’ll be ready to go by training camp or early in training camp.”
- Eagle's star veteran WR AJ Brown is NOT present for Day 1 of Philadelphia's OTAs. This was somewhat expected as the trade rumors continue to potentially solidify as we head to June-1st.
- With the Chief's down their 1 & 2 presumably WRs in Rashee Rice and Xavier Worthy, HC Andy Reid was seen "coaching up" 2nd-year, 4th Rd pick WR Jalen Royals in KC's 1st day of OTAs. The sense is that although Royals did struggle and dealt with injuries in his rookie season, the hope is for a year two leap from Royals if the WR is limited to begin the season.
- With legal troubles swirling around Packer's RB Josh Jacobs, it was year-2 RB MarShawn Lloyd who was seen running 1st in positional drills during GB's first day of OTA practices with Jacobs absent due to his first appearance in court. Chris Brooks was in after Lloyd while also alternating with the 1's.
- IND starting QB Daniel Jones, who is coming off a torn Achilles during the 2025 NFL season, was spotted doing some individual Positional drills in day 1 of OTAs. Positive sign for the recovery phase of Jones' process. Was also seen running full field wind sprints after practice concluded.
- When asked who has been "stepping it up in a big way" so far in the offseason + OTAs, MIA RB DeVon Achane cited: "Malik Washington". 🚀
- Falcons HC Kevin Stefanski on QB Tua Tagovailoa: "His accuracy is an innate, God-given ability". Very high praise for the newcomer from Miami to start OTAs. Stefanski also asked about QB Michael Penix progression so far during his ACL injury rehab: "He is not cleared for 11v11 year, but he is exactly where he needs to be."
- Chargers HC Jim Harbaugh on his TE room of Njoku/Gadsden II: "The athleticism, the catch radius, the speed, they're very similar in pass catching and route running. Can't have enough good players." Heavily agree with the sentiment here, the entire QB2 to QB18 range clustering in ADP has made some of the elites a lot more enticing (has led me to heavy Maye and JD5 exposure).

I don’t have the numbers for past year ADP on UD but feel like the ~QB8-12 range is also quite boosted up from years past (mid round 7 to R8). Drafting those guys so close to QB2-5 feels pretty gross, when you can get much higher upside bets a round or two earlier and very similarly projecting QBs beyond round 8 (Purdy, Goff, Love, Nix, etc).

QB is obviously team dependent and you’ll have guys you like in various ranges, but I’ve made it a point to avoid most of that QB8-12 range unless they fall.

The Kincaid price continuing to tank has been pretty surprising to me. Could very well be the best/leading receiver on a Josh Allen-led offense. 

The route share concerns are real, and he'll likely never get close to a top tier % at the position, but you'd have to imagine the injury issues had been playing into the extremely low route rate from last year to some degree (44.2% last year, over 60% avg in 2 years prior). 

Route concerns seem more than baked in at an 11th/12th round price tag, for the most efficient TE in football last year (0.27 TPRR, 3.19 YPRR!) with top-tier TD upside in the Bills offense. I think we can see him get back up to even ~65% route share, as that seems pretty likely given his route shares the 2 years prior, and that he has improved as a receiver too. At that improved route share, even if the efficiency dips, the upside seems quite high for the TE13 in ADP and he seems like a good bet to easily outperform that.

My honest-to-goodness evaluation of Caleb Williams:

He improved dramatically YoY and made the playoffs last year. But objectively, he hasn't played the position at a high level. I'd say he's been well below-average in both years.

He can make insane "wow" throws only a few other QBs can make. That's really cool! But it's also massively overrated. These are rare events that matter significantly less than being a consistently accurate passer who avoids negative plays. Highlight reel throws stick in the back of the mind of the average fan, who weights them too heavily as if one sick throw is the same as making 99/100 easy completions or cancels out 3 turnover-worthy throws plus 6 sacks. It doesn't. Not even close.

From a real NFL standpoint, I do think he's overrated and that his PR has been much better than he's deserved. But I still really like him from a fantasy perspective. Chicago's supporting cast (including Ben Johnson calling the plays) almost feels like an impossible-to-fail setup, or even an impossible-not-to-be-great situation.

Caleb Williams finished as the QB8 last year (19.2 FPG) despite ranking bottom-5 in every QB accuracy stat imaginable. What happens if he's closer to average this year? Or what if he's actually really good?

I’m a big Tuten bull but can’t get on board with Dobbins not meaning anything if he had signed with JAX. Him and C-Rod are not the same caliber RB, so I think Tuten’s outlook could’ve been much different/worse with Dobbins there.

That being said, they of course didn’t sign Dobbins. And that makes me feel even better about Tuten now. C-Rod was seemingly less of a priority than many are making him out to be (I don’t think Coen coaching him in college 5 years ago proves anything at all), and he’s nowhere near the pedigree of Dobbins. His signing, IMO, makes the Tuten breakout much more likely than it would’ve been w/ Dobbins.

I don’t think JAX’s plans for Tuten have  to be as binary as they’re being perceived, in that their plans w/ Tuten alongside Dobbins could’ve been much different than their current plans for him.
