BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/26:

- As expected, 3rd-year TB RB Bucky Irving is NOT present on the field at OTAs as he is still recovering and rehabbing on injured shoulder that required surgery. HC Todd Bowles when asked about Irvings return: "Sometime in the summer or fall". 😕
- TEN QB Cam Ward on 1st-Rd Rookie Carnell Tate after first week of OTAs: "He'll have an explosive year just because he'll get a lot of one-on-one matchups. That's what we want. We want guys to come down and play, man, because we think we can beat him." 🚀
- 3rd-Year HOU TE Cade Stover has begun taking reps at FB while also serving as TE depth for the Texans. The Ohio State product has yet to play a full season due to injuries and is looking for a way to remain on the roster after cut-down time competing with the likes of Brevin Jordan, Foster Moreau, and Harrison Bryant.
- RUMOR MILL: Could Veteran SF WR Brandon Aiyuk be a low-risk, high-reward match to the New York Giants, who could possibly be without Malik Nabers to begin the 2026 season?
-  NY Jets want to give rookie QB Cade Klubnik a "good look and evaluation" before making any final decisions on signing a back-up veteran QB behind starter Geno Smith.
- Bear's QB Coach J.T. Barrett on emphasis for year three for starting QB Caleb Williams: "More efficiency, Less Superhero Plays". 
- Pelissero expects "fair competition going in" for the Minnesota QBs Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy heading into training camp. “If this were just, it’s Kyler’s job, and J.J. is competing with Carson Wentz to be the two, you would have traded J.J."
- Per @AlbertBreer: Chiefs, Chargers, Bills and Rams all had their shot at this already at AJ Brown and are all likely out." Stating the obvious also, what is most likely scenario is a reunion with Mike Vrabel.
- Raider's HC Klint Kubiak reiterates once again after second day of OTAs pertaining to 2nd year stud RB Ashton Jeanty on a potential snap split in the backfield: "I don't know the play snap percentage, but you look at Christian McCaffrey, his play snap percentage is high. So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀
- CAR RB Jonathon Brooks participating and looks to be a full go in early OTAs, reports are that he has "bulked up" a little and is already flashing nicely on run and passing game.

Why I said this - yes, Breece Hall is absolutely going to get the highest share of backfield touches in this Jet's offensive backfield.

2025 Week 1 Snaps: Breece (58%), Allen (31%), Davis (13%)

2025 Week 2: Breece (64%), Allen (19%), Davis (21%)

2025 Week 3: Breece (55%), Allen (33%), Davis (13%)

2025 Week 4: Breece (64%), Allen (10% got injured), Davis (33%)

2025 Week 5: Breece (60%), Davis (40%)

You can basically count on Breece playing anywhere from 55-65% of snaps if all backs are healthy. Again, he will dominate touches, but he isn't "bell cow" territory and that is exactly what AG said last year as well and the Snap% showed it was going to be basically 60/40 split. Breece's price is still a bit up there now (going around King, Jeremiyah Love, Chase Brown, Travis ETN), but he was much cheaper before the contract ext. I caution paying an early 3rd-Round pick at the moment.

Tough for me to get behind the Saints being a better situation for Etienne. The pace stuff and improvements to the Saints offense is a plus, but the Jags had that in 2025 and more.

JAX was 9th in plays per game last year (NO 11th, but led in sec/play), and was top 12 in EPA/Play (NO 27th, although should improve). They also had a strong offensive line as they were t-6th in ESPN run block win rate (NO t-21st), and have Liam Coen running the show who has continually proven to be a top tier run game-designer.

Something I think that tends to get lost on people (myself included at times) is the concept of sample sizes. Not just with Tuten of course, but he’s a great example. 

I often see the citing of his poor explosive rush rate (15+ yards runs) as a rookie being used to declare that he isn’t good at creating big plays on the ground. 

The guy had 83 carries last year, with one of the highest rates of short yardage rushing in the NFL. That’s a very small sample size, and you’d be much better off looking at his college profile + top end speed to realize there’s plenty of upside for him to be an explosive rusher. 

Does that mean he will definitely improve and be a major hit in year 2? No, but his explosive rate in year 1 is hardly a reason to discredit him. And the Jags letting go Etienne to only bring in Chris Rodriguez is quite the vote of confidence in Tuten.

BIGGEST NFL News 5/21 (OTAs):

- Fresh off a new mega-max contract extension, Dolphin's star RB DeVon Achane is apparently already dealing with an (undisclosed) injury with absence in Miami's first OTAs segment. 😬
- LAC GM Joe Hortiz reports in interview, "The door is not closed on veteran WR Keenan Allen".
- Titans HC Robert Salah says, "Cam Wards arm is "perfectly healthy".
- Early reports out of Brown's camp is that new HC Todd Monken and veteran QB Deshaun Watson have allegedly "hit it off" early in OTAs. Watson has been taking all of the 1st and 2nd team reps early on.
- Giants HC John Harbaugh reported that WR Darius Slayton recently had surgery for a "sports Hernia" but still feels he will be 100% for the start of training camp.
- KC Chiefs star WR Rashee Rice has been sentenced to a mandatory stay of 30 days in the Dallas County jail for a probation violation due to failing a test for marijuana. 
- KC WR Rashee Rice has "clean up" surgery on his injured right knee to help with inflammation. He will rehab the knee while serving his 30 days in Dallas County jail and stands to be sidelined for roughly 2 months with the rehabbing.
- NYG Stud WR Malik Nabers recently had "clean up" surgery on the same knee he tore his ACL & meniscus during Week 4 of the 2025 NFL season. HC Harbaugh reported, "he is going through the slog of his recovery, and the goal is to still have Nabers out there in Week 1, but it is not a simple recovery". 🙃
- Bills HC Joe Brady reiterates that "Keon Coleman is here to be a part of this offense". Coleman labeled his second year in the NFL as "make or break".
- Veteran QB Kirk Cousins receives ALL of 1st-team reps in Raider's OTAs, followed by Aiden O'Connell, and 3rd-team reps with rookie 1st overall pick Fernando Mendoza. Although, Mendoza has already "stood out".
- 2nd year CLE RB Quinshon Judkins wows Twitter in clip of OTAs catching a pass and quickly turning up field in a dead sprint, looking WELL ahead of his recovery from a broken fibula suffered late last season. 👀
- NYG RB Cam Skattebo tells reporters that he expects to have 300 carries and over 2,000 yards in his 2nd year in the NFL. Skattebo is also recovery from a fractured ankle + fibula and says he will be ready for Week 1. 
- Falcons HC Kevin Stefanski says the 1st team reps being split down the middle for both QBs Tua Tagovailoa and QB Michael Penix in OTAs is "intentional".
- Raven's Star QB Lamar Jackson "not present" at voluntary OTAs on Tuesday.
- Veteran Cardinals QB Jacoby Brissett is not attending Arizona's OTAs, as he is currently seeking a pay raise to be the starting QB for the team in 2026.
- Future HOF QB Aaron Rodgers signs new 1-year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers for up to $25M with incentives. Rodgers reunites with old ball coach Mike McCarthy for what he says will be his final year before retiring. 🙌
- New WAS Commanders OC David Blough persistently states that he wants to get veteran star wide-out Terry McLaurin upwards of 10 targets per game in 2026. 🚀
- The Detroit Lions have officially extended star LB Jack Campbell to a new 4-year contract that will put Campbell with the franchise past 2030.
- High praise from Niners QB Brock Purdy on rookie De'Zhaun Stribling saying, "feel like Stribling is gonna be a guy who comes in and makes an instant impact." 
- Per Adam Schefter, "Tyreek Hill is an injury (Ankle) situation where he might not be ready until midway through the season” 😮
- New Raider's HC Klint Kubiak on 3rd year stud TE Brock Bowers: "He is a football robot from Heaven".
- ATL Falcons 3rd round rookie WR Zachariah Branch has "big day" in first day of open OTAs making "the most explosive play in the Falcons’ offense vs. defense period."
- Titans Beat: Rookie standout WR Carnell Tate INSTANT winner of Titan's first day of OTAs. Citing multiple TD catches from 2nd year QB Cam Ward, working long and short, catching balls all over the field, both open and contested. Looking "as advertised".

“Slayton is expected to be ready for training camp, and his contract makes him untouchable, even if his role is undetermined.”

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. On Josh Jacobs: 
* Under the Personal Conduct Policy, the NFL will conduct its own investigation
* The NFL can suspend a player even if player has not been convicted (doesn't even have to be charged)
* Baseline suspension for domestic violence is 6 games, although that can be altered based on mitigating factors
* Impossible to know how long investigation will take -- without video evidence or fast moves from prosecutors, NFL could wait for court developments to play out
* The NFL can also use the Commissioner Exempt List (paid leave) in situations a player is charged with a violent crime

Forgot to do this after walking back home last night but here are some knee-jerk reactions to Game 2 of the NBA Finals:

1. Dylan Harper's ascension from elite roleplayer to starter in this league lies in his playmaking. It's only sporadically been a problem this season but the vision and timing of passing out of traffic in Game 2 was quite a letdown.

2. Victor Wembanyama desperately needs a postup game. He's been the worse starting center in both games this series and one way to not let mediocre defensive matchups off the hook is improving his 1v1 game from the elbow.

3. Mike Brown's drive-and-kick offense, which he was a pioneer of with the Warriors, has been on full display in this series. Good teams become great by generating spotup threes in this way—it reminds me why Mike Malone being without a head coach job right now is one of the league's best-kept secrets.

Chase Brown is one of my biggest fades in drafts right now. He was indeed splitting work 60/40 with Perine when Perine was active from Week 5 on. 

It wasn’t just low-value work Perine took, the two split goal line rushes evenly in this time. Brown was able to get there on receiving volume and running extremely hot on receiving TDs down the stretch (5 in final 4 games), but that’s unsustainable for an RB (or even a WR/TE). And his raw target numbers, which were strong but inflated some by negative game scripts, could come down as well as we’re expecting a much more competitive Bengals team in 2026.

I think he belongs in the Jacobs/Breece tier of ADP rather than Henry/Walker group. He’s a guy propped up more by theoretical role over raw talent (middling efficiency, explosive rates and rushing production each of L2 years), not a bet I want to be making regardless this early but especially considering the role is probably a little overrated.

Admittedly, I'm a sucker for Ben Brown, so when he pitches well, it's partly confirmation bias. Brown threw a quality start against the Pirates on Monday across an efficient 83 pitches. He elicited a 19.3% swinging-strike rate with a 30% ball rate on Monday. 

🪝Brown's knuckle curve was cooking, given his 31.4% swinging-strike rate, with most of them coming below the zone. The knuckle curve led his arsenal by throwing them 53% of the time to right-handed hitters, up from 37.7% (2026) and 42.7% (2025). 
⏩He threw his four-seam and sinker in the zone 56% of the time in 2026. However, Brown was pumping sinkers and four-seamers 70-71% of the time on Monday. The sinker generated weak contact (71.7 mph average exit velocity), but his four-seam was hit hard (105.4 mph EV). Thankfully, both fastballs generated several called and first-pitch strikes. 

This was Brown's second game in 2026 with 15 or more whiffs. We love seeing him adjust his approach as a starting pitcher in 2026.

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/27:

- Packers 3x Pro-Bowl, 1x All-Pro RB Josh Jacobs was arrested and booked into the Brown County jail upon allegations of FIVE charges (2 potential felonies) including battery/domestic abuse, criminal damage to property, disorderly conduct, strangulation & suffocation, and intimidation of a victim. Since, Jacobs has been released from detainment with no formal charges filed yet as investigation continues.
- Seattle HC Mike Macdonald provides a positive note on 2nd-year WR Tory Horton, who looks to potentially make a return to the playing field after suffering a leg/shin injury by the end of Spring.
- Great News out of KC as QB Patrick Mahomes was on the field working out in some capacity for the first day of the Chief's OTAs. 
- The 49ers and highly sought back-up QB Mac Jones have agreed on a revised contract that will add roughly $300,000 roster bonus to Jones' currently compensation. Jones is now set to make $3.55m base with another $2.25m up for grabs in incentives.
- Jaguar's HC Liam Coen on his RB Room: "It's a group that has a lot of opportunities open to them. There's a wide-open room. It's truly wide open."👀
- Journeyman ARI QB Jacoby Brissett continues his hold-out by still being non-present in Cardinal's on-going OTAs. Brissett is still looking for a revised/raised contract to be the team's starting QB.
- New Dolphin's HC Jeff Halsey says that RB DeVon Achane is still recovering from shoulder "clean up" surgery from injury sustained at the end of last season and isn't participating (fully) in OTAs as a result.
- 1st Day of OTAs in Minnesota, looks like 2nd-year Vikings QB J.J. McCarthy has cleaned up his footwork/throwing mechanics' while seemingly taking 1st team reps per Beat.
- KC Chief's WR Xavier Worthy "not ahead of schedule" pertaining to his recovery from a torn labrum in the early part of last season. Seen wearing a yellow "non-contact/injured" jersey in day 1 of OTAs.
- Packers HC Matt LaFleur on TE Tucker Kraft as he rehabs from ACL surgery recovery: “He looks so good, you’d want to put him out there, hopefully he’ll be ready to go by training camp or early in training camp.”
- Eagle's star veteran WR AJ Brown is NOT present for Day 1 of Philadelphia's OTAs. This was somewhat expected as the trade rumors continue to potentially solidify as we head to June-1st.
- With the Chief's down their 1 & 2 presumably WRs in Rashee Rice and Xavier Worthy, HC Andy Reid was seen "coaching up" 2nd-year, 4th Rd pick WR Jalen Royals in KC's 1st day of OTAs. The sense is that although Royals did struggle and dealt with injuries in his rookie season, the hope is for a year two leap from Royals if the WR is limited to begin the season.
- With legal troubles swirling around Packer's RB Josh Jacobs, it was year-2 RB MarShawn Lloyd who was seen running 1st in positional drills during GB's first day of OTA practices with Jacobs absent due to his first appearance in court. Chris Brooks was in after Lloyd while also alternating with the 1's.
- IND starting QB Daniel Jones, who is coming off a torn Achilles during the 2025 NFL season, was spotted doing some individual Positional drills in day 1 of OTAs. Positive sign for the recovery phase of Jones' process. Was also seen running full field wind sprints after practice concluded.
- When asked who has been "stepping it up in a big way" so far in the offseason + OTAs, MIA RB DeVon Achane cited: "Malik Washington". 🚀
- Falcons HC Kevin Stefanski on QB Tua Tagovailoa: "His accuracy is an innate, God-given ability". Very high praise for the newcomer from Miami to start OTAs. Stefanski also asked about QB Michael Penix progression so far during his ACL injury rehab: "He is not cleared for 11v11 year, but he is exactly where he needs to be."
- Chargers HC Jim Harbaugh on his TE room of Njoku/Gadsden II: "The athleticism, the catch radius, the speed, they're very similar in pass catching and route running. Can't have enough good players." Harbaugh was also asked about his WRs, stated "Quientin Johnston, Tre' Harris, and Ladd McConkey have all looked elite in phases 1&2".

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

I’m struggling with the price of De’Von Achane at the 1/2 turn.

For one, you have the major offensive environment concern as the Dolphins could very well be the worst offense in the league. Scoring opps could be low, which is particularly concerning for a guy who already doesn't get as many goal line rushes as we'd like (38.5% inside the 5 % is outside top 30 RBs). 

And Achane's efficiency could plummet again like in 2024 (outside top 18 RBs in YPC AND explosive rate per @FantasyPtsData).

He also goes from check-down merchant Tua to one of the most rushing-prone QBs in the league in Willis. A lot of his value comes from those targets out of the backfield, which I worry could be vastly reduced in 2026 (top 4 in RB targets each of L2 years).

Achane's talent (both in general and relative to his teammates) could end up winning out at this range of the drafts, but he seemingly just has a lot more to fight through to hit a massive season compared to some of these other guys. I prefer all of Jeanty, Ken Walker, James Cook, and Saquon at this 1/2 turn range.
Good for Marshawn Lloyd. Hoping for the best.

On Josh Jacobs: 
* Under the Personal Conduct Policy, the NFL will conduct its own investigation
* The NFL can suspend a player even if player has not been convicted (doesn't even have to be charged)
* Baseline suspension for domestic violence is 6 games, although that can be altered based on mitigating factors
* Impossible to know how long investigation will take -- without video evidence or fast moves from prosecutors, NFL could wait for court developments to play out
* The NFL can also use the Commissioner Exempt List (paid leave) in situations a player is charged with a violent crime

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/27:

- Packers 3x Pro-Bowl, 1x All-Pro RB Josh Jacobs was arrested and booked into the Brown County jail upon allegations of FIVE charges (2 potential felonies) including battery/domestic abuse, criminal damage to property, disorderly conduct, strangulation & suffocation, and intimidation of a victim. Since, Jacobs has been released from detainment with no formal charges filed yet as investigation continues.
- Seattle HC Mike Macdonald provides a positive note on 2nd-year WR Tory Horton, who looks to potentially make a return to the playing field after suffering a leg/shin injury by the end of Spring.
- Great News out of KC as QB Patrick Mahomes was on the field working out in some capacity for the first day of the Chief's OTAs. 
- The 49ers and highly sought back-up QB Mac Jones have agreed on a revised contract that will add roughly $300,000 roster bonus to Jones' currently compensation. Jones is now set to make $3.55m base with another $2.25m up for grabs in incentives.
- Jaguar's HC Liam Coen on his RB Room: "It's a group that has a lot of opportunities open to them. There's a wide-open room. It's truly wide open."👀
- Journeyman ARI QB Jacoby Brissett continues his hold-out by still being non-present in Cardinal's on-going OTAs. Brissett is still looking for a revised/raised contract to be the team's starting QB.
- New Dolphin's HC Jeff Halsey says that RB DeVon Achane is still recovering from shoulder "clean up" surgery from injury sustained at the end of last season and isn't participating (fully) in OTAs as a result.
- 1st Day of OTAs in Minnesota, looks like 2nd-year Vikings QB J.J. McCarthy has cleaned up his footwork/throwing mechanics' while seemingly taking 1st team reps per Beat.
- KC Chief's WR Xavier Worthy "not ahead of schedule" pertaining to his recovery from a torn labrum in the early part of last season. Seen wearing a yellow "non-contact/injured" jersey in day 1 of OTAs.
- Packers HC Matt LaFleur on TE Tucker Kraft as he rehabs from ACL surgery recovery: “He looks so good, you’d want to put him out there, hopefully he’ll be ready to go by training camp or early in training camp.”
- Eagle's star veteran WR AJ Brown is NOT present for Day 1 of Philadelphia's OTAs. This was somewhat expected as the trade rumors continue to potentially solidify as we head to June-1st.
- With the Chief's down their 1 & 2 presumably WRs in Rashee Rice and Xavier Worthy, HC Andy Reid was seen "coaching up" 2nd-year, 4th Rd pick WR Jalen Royals in KC's 1st day of OTAs. The sense is that although Royals did struggle and dealt with injuries in his rookie season, the hope is for a year two leap from Royals if the WR is limited to begin the season.
- With legal troubles swirling around Packer's RB Josh Jacobs, it was year-2 RB MarShawn Lloyd who was seen running 1st in positional drills during GB's first day of OTA practices with Jacobs absent due to his first appearance in court. Chris Brooks was in after Lloyd while also alternating with the 1's.
- IND starting QB Daniel Jones, who is coming off a torn Achilles during the 2025 NFL season, was spotted doing some individual Positional drills in day 1 of OTAs. Positive sign for the recovery phase of Jones' process. Was also seen running full field wind sprints after practice concluded.
- When asked who has been "stepping it up in a big way" so far in the offseason + OTAs, MIA RB DeVon Achane cited: "Malik Washington". 🚀
- Falcons HC Kevin Stefanski on QB Tua Tagovailoa: "His accuracy is an innate, God-given ability". Very high praise for the newcomer from Miami to start OTAs. Stefanski also asked about QB Michael Penix progression so far during his ACL injury rehab: "He is not cleared for 11v11 year, but he is exactly where he needs to be."
- Chargers HC Jim Harbaugh on his TE room of Njoku/Gadsden II: "The athleticism, the catch radius, the speed, they're very similar in pass catching and route running. Can't have enough good players." Harbaugh was also asked about his WRs, stated "Quientin Johnston, Tre' Harris, and Ladd McConkey have all looked elite in phases 1&2".

- New Eagles OC Sean Mannion on Rookie WR Makai Lemon: “Makai is very impressive, he’s a tremendous young talent, takes a lot of pride in the little detail. With his degree of talent and potential the sky is the limit".
- TEN 2nd-Year QB Cameron Ward has shed 10 pounds during the offseason but watching his calories and upping his cardio regime. Dare we say, "Best shape of his life" SZN is HERE? 😜
- Commanders HC Dan Quinn on rookie WR Antonio Williams: "Has absolutely jumped out. As a route runner, he can stop, he can change directions, explosives, this guy’s got real natural feel as a receiver. Antonio has it, man, in the biggest way.” 🔥
- New Ravens OC Declan Doyle "Taking Note", of rookie standout WR Ja'Kobi Lane after first week of Baltimore's OTAs. Not only has Lane provided immense energy/personality during OTAs, on the field is where he has really shown the most.
- Veteran NYG WR Darnell Mooney on his role with the Giants for the 2026 NFL Season: "my role is to make plays and dominate."
- BUF TE Dalton Kincaid heading into year four of his NFL career, says "This is the most confident I have felt both physically and mentally since entering the league". Kincaid has dealt with multiple injuries over the last two seasons including knee and PCL issues, but all signs point to positive outlook for 2026.

My honest-to-goodness evaluation of Caleb Williams:

He improved dramatically YoY and made the playoffs last year. But objectively, he hasn't played the position at a high level. I'd say he's been well below-average in both years.

He can make insane "wow" throws only a few other QBs can make. That's really cool! But it's also massively overrated. These are rare events that matter significantly less than being a consistently accurate passer who avoids negative plays. Highlight reel throws stick in the back of the mind of the average fan, who weights them too heavily as if one sick throw is the same as making 99/100 easy completions or cancels out 3 turnover-worthy throws plus 6 sacks. It doesn't. Not even close.

From a real NFL standpoint, I do think he's overrated and that his PR has been much better than he's deserved. But I still really like him from a fantasy perspective. Chicago's supporting cast (including Ben Johnson calling the plays) almost feels like an impossible-to-fail setup, or even an impossible-not-to-be-great situation.

Caleb Williams finished as the QB8 last year (19.2 FPG) despite ranking bottom-5 in every QB accuracy stat imaginable. What happens if he's closer to average this year? Or what if he's actually really good?

Trevor Lawrence made some wildly impressive throws in 2025. We always knew the arm talent was top-notch, but it didn’t all come together until paired up with Liam Coen.

Coen empowered him to:

- use his legs more as a playmaker
- trust his reads (pre-snap motion to give intel on defensive looks + route concepts designed to confirm/attack post-snap)
- unlock all areas of the field (career-high middle-field throw rate in 2025)

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/26:

- As expected, 3rd-year TB RB Bucky Irving is NOT present on the field at OTAs as he is still recovering and rehabbing on injured shoulder that required surgery. HC Todd Bowles when asked about Irvings return: "Sometime in the summer or fall". 😕
- TEN QB Cam Ward on 1st-Rd Rookie Carnell Tate after first week of OTAs: "He'll have an explosive year just because he'll get a lot of one-on-one matchups. That's what we want. We want guys to come down and play, man, because we think we can beat him." 🚀
- 3rd-Year HOU TE Cade Stover has begun taking reps at FB while also serving as TE depth for the Texans. The Ohio State product has yet to play a full season due to injuries and is looking for a way to remain on the roster after cut-down time competing with the likes of Brevin Jordan, Foster Moreau, and Harrison Bryant.
- RUMOR MILL: Could Veteran SF WR Brandon Aiyuk be a low-risk, high-reward match to the New York Giants, who could possibly be without Malik Nabers to begin the 2026 season?
-  NY Jets want to give rookie QB Cade Klubnik a "good look and evaluation" before making any final decisions on signing a back-up veteran QB behind starter Geno Smith.
- Bear's QB Coach J.T. Barrett on emphasis for year three for starting QB Caleb Williams: "More efficiency, Less Superhero Plays". 
- Pelissero expects "fair competition going in" for the Minnesota QBs Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy heading into training camp. “If this were just, it’s Kyler’s job, and J.J. is competing with Carson Wentz to be the two, you would have traded J.J."
- Per @AlbertBreer: Chiefs, Chargers, Bills and Rams all had their shot at this already at AJ Brown and are all likely out." Stating the obvious also, what is most likely scenario is a reunion with Mike Vrabel.
- Raider's HC Klint Kubiak reiterates once again after second day of OTAs pertaining to 2nd year stud RB Ashton Jeanty on a potential snap split in the backfield: "I don't know the play snap percentage, but you look at Christian McCaffrey, his play snap percentage is high. So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀
- CAR RB Jonathon Brooks participating and looks to be a full go in early OTAs, reports are that he has "bulked up" a little and is already flashing nicely on run and passing game.
- Denver HC Sean Payton was asked "Who is the WR1 between veteran Courtland Sutton and newcomer Jaylen Waddle?", his response: "We’re not just going to have. So, we don’t have [that]. We just play different packages and try to give those guys the things that they do well.”
- The Houston Texans and star WR Nico Collins agreed on a contract negotiation/adjustment that guarantees Collins' salary for the next two NFL seasons, while also getting a pay increase of $8m for this season and $9m for next season. 
- 2nd-Year Jag's WR/CB Travis Hunter is indeed participating in Jaguar's OTAs this week. Was seen doing something quite unspeakable today - catching a pass on a WR route from QB Trevor Lawrence. We thought he only played CB now.
- Jacksonville HC Liam Coen was asked about why newly signed RB Chris Rodriguez Jr. was NOT in attendance for first day of OTAs: "Yeah, you know I mean, the injury report will come out at some point." 
- Per @Schultz_Report, Arizona Cardinals have fielded calls from teams around the league pertaining to trade discussions centered around Pro-Bowl DE/EDGE Josh Sweat.
- 3rd-year TB WR Jalen McMillan on excitement of opening the playbook under new OC Zac Robinson: "We had a lot of plays last year [under Josh Grizzard] that weren't called, but right now we are repping a lot of them and giving the coaches confidence in calling them."
- Jaguar's OC Grant Udinksi gushes over WR Parker Washington in Post-OTA interview: "I think really, really highly of Parker. I hope Parker thinks really, really highly of himself, because we think that he is capable of what he did at the end of the season last year and even more than that." 🚀

Quotes 👇

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

On Josh Jacobs: 
* Under the Personal Conduct Policy, the NFL will conduct its own investigation
* The NFL can suspend a player even if player has not been convicted (doesn't even have to be charged)
* Baseline suspension for domestic violence is 6 games, although that can be altered based on mitigating factors
* Impossible to know how long investigation will take -- without video evidence or fast moves from prosecutors, NFL could wait for court developments to play out
* The NFL can also use the Commissioner Exempt List (paid leave) in situations a player is charged with a violent crime

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

1.02 by an absolute mile for me. Personally don't think either Harvey or the 1.08 are even half as valuable as the 1.02, making it a senseless trade down IMO.

+ 1.02 - Tate/Tyson get you a day WR2 in dynasty value, with clear upside to be WR1s relatively quickly.

Vs.

+ 1.08 - Assuming Sadiq here, low-end TE1 that could end up buried as the 3rd or 4th target in a potentially really bad offense for multiple years. Possible F-TE only with Mason Taylor there for a while, and poor prospect relative to where he got drafted too. Massive drop-off after 1.07 for me.

+ RJ Harvey - Basically a throw in at this point, seems like a pretty stark underdog to every take on a lead role w/o injury. Was really bad in year 1 as a rusher and is already 25.

My honest-to-goodness evaluation of Caleb Williams:

He improved dramatically YoY and made the playoffs last year. But objectively, he hasn't played the position at a high level. I'd say he's been well below-average in both years.

He can make insane "wow" throws only a few other QBs can make. That's really cool! But it's also massively overrated. These are rare events that matter significantly less than being a consistently accurate passer who avoids negative plays. Highlight reel throws stick in the back of the mind of the average fan, who weights them too heavily as if one sick throw is the same as making 99/100 easy completions or cancels out 3 turnover-worthy throws plus 6 sacks. It doesn't. Not even close.

From a real NFL standpoint, I do think he's overrated and that his PR has been much better than he's deserved. But I still really like him from a fantasy perspective. Chicago's supporting cast (including Ben Johnson calling the plays) almost feels like an impossible-to-fail setup, or even an impossible-not-to-be-great situation.

Caleb Williams finished as the QB8 last year (19.2 FPG) despite ranking bottom-5 in every QB accuracy stat imaginable. What happens if he's closer to average this year? Or what if he's actually really good?

Trevor Lawrence made some wildly impressive throws in 2025. We always knew the arm talent was top-notch, but it didn’t all come together until paired up with Liam Coen.

Coen empowered him to:

- use his legs more as a playmaker
- trust his reads (pre-snap motion to give intel on defensive looks + route concepts designed to confirm/attack post-snap)
- unlock all areas of the field (career-high middle-field throw rate in 2025)

On Josh Jacobs: 
* Under the Personal Conduct Policy, the NFL will conduct its own investigation
* The NFL can suspend a player even if player has not been convicted (doesn't even have to be charged)
* Baseline suspension for domestic violence is 6 games, although that can be altered based on mitigating factors
* Impossible to know how long investigation will take -- without video evidence or fast moves from prosecutors, NFL could wait for court developments to play out
* The NFL can also use the Commissioner Exempt List (paid leave) in situations a player is charged with a violent crime

These conversations of 'wait till the best athletes in the US play soccer' are silly

Both those players were great athletes, but neither one was a great player BECAUSE they were a great athlete.

Ultimately, being a great athlete will always help, but it won't make anyone great (player or national pathway) until the technical ability is there or developed.

If 2 soccer players have the same technical and mental level, take the better athlete, but arguably the best 2 players of all time (Messi and Maradona) were/are both marginal athletes. Their technical level was/is absurd and they saw the game better.

I’m a big Tuten bull but can’t get on board with Dobbins not meaning anything if he had signed with JAX. Him and C-Rod are not the same caliber RB, so I think Tuten’s outlook could’ve been much different/worse with Dobbins there.

That being said, they of course didn’t sign Dobbins. And that makes me feel even better about Tuten now. C-Rod was seemingly less of a priority than many are making him out to be (I don’t think Coen coaching him in college 5 years ago proves anything at all), and he’s nowhere near the pedigree of Dobbins. His signing, IMO, makes the Tuten breakout much more likely than it would’ve been w/ Dobbins.

I don’t think JAX’s plans for Tuten have  to be as binary as they’re being perceived, in that their plans w/ Tuten alongside Dobbins could’ve been much different than their current plans for him.

Los Angeles Dodgers' starting pitcher Roki Sasaki had his 4th game with 15 or more whiffs since May 1. Sasaki elicited a 17.8% swinging-strike rate and a 30% ball rate on Friday against the Orioles. 

⏩Sasaki was throwing his four-seamer in the zone 65% of the time, with a 40% called strike rate and a 77% stirke rate. His heater elicited an 18.6% swinging-strike rate on Friday, with a mixture of fastballs in the heart and in the upper third of the zone. Sasaki was throwing his four-seamer over 1 mph harder (+1.3 mph) on Friday.
🪓All of Sasaki's splitter whiffs (5) were thrown low and below the zone, leading to a 17.9% swinging-strike rate, 39% zone rate, and a 53% chase rate. For context, Sasaki's splitter swinging-strike rate (18.4%) has been similar to Friday. Sasaki's splitter has a 41.9% zone rate and a 35.6% chase rate against left-handed hitters.

I've been highly intrigued by Sasaki in shallow to medium-sized leagues.

I wrote about Houston Astros' starting pitcher Tatsuya Imai as a pitcher to monitor if he improves his stuff and command. Imai threw a quality start with a season-high 11 strikeouts against the Guardians. 

🔥Imai set a season-high with 20 whiffs on Friday, leading to a 22.5% swinging-strike rate and a 36% ball rate against the Guardians. He hit 17+ whiffs in three other starts this season. That's significantly better than Imai's 41% ball rate and 12.2% swinging-strike this season before Friday.
🛝Imai threw his gyro-like slider harder (+1.5 mph), eliciting a 34.1% swinging-strike rate against the Guardians. Nearly all his slider whiffs were low and below the zone, aligning with his 40% zone rate, 50% chase rate, and 71% strike rate. The Guardians stacked lefties (44%) on Friday, nearly identical to his usage against left-handed hitters (43.5%). 

Imai's 8.3% K-BB% and 12.1% swinging-strike rate tell us it will be a bumpy ride. He intrigues us, but it might be more unpredictable than we would like.

LATE RD TEAM Stacks/Picks (1-3 players) You SHOULD be Clicking in Best Ball Contests:

- Miami Dolphins: WR/TE room fully OPEN for targets without a current front-runner. Newly drafted rookies Chris Bell & Caleb Douglas bring their own skill, but also have Jalen Tolbert (X-style, big RZ target), Tutu Atwell (speed, long-ball route runner), and Malik Washington (can play Slot/Z role for short/intermediate routes). Greg Dulcic signed a 1-year deal and seems to be the lead TE at moment.

- Atlanta Falcons: Behind Drake London and Kyle Pitts, the WR2-3-4 is absolutely undetermined at the moment. Falcons signed both vet WRs Olamide Zaccheaus & Jahan Dotson, while drafting shifty slot-style WR in Zachariah Branch out of Georgia. Really not a great comp behind Kyle Pitts at TE, even though there have been talks of potential trades. Taking shots on the three WR's above in Branch, Zaccheaus, & Dotson likely presents some nice options that could turn into weekly lineup players behind London.

- Las Vegas Raiders: This is such an easy one to get a back-stack in since you can literally get both Mendoza and presumable starter Kirk Cousins pretty late in drafts. Behind Brock Bowers, there will be LOTS of open target competition. The Raiders brought in Jalen Nailor (3-years, $35m), while already having Tre Tucker, Jack Bech, and Dont'e Thornton on the active roster. Needless to say, all three of the first mentioned should vie heavily for snaps and targets (barring no adds prior to season). Also, Mike Washington Jr. is one of the better contingent backs behind stud Ashton Jeanty, and you can also get him very late.

- New York Giants: The Giants also present one of the easier ways to get late-rd stacks if you decide to take Jaxson Dart in the earlier rounds. You have a plethora of options given the current outlook that star WR Malik Nabers is "more than likely" not going to be on the field for week 1 and could even hit the PUP for the first four weeks. The Giants signed on both Darnell Mooney & Calvin Austin III, while already having Darius Slayton and Jaylin Hyatt on the roster. They also took a stance drafting Notre Dame standout Malachi Fields. The Giants ran a lot of two-TE sets last season so Theo Johnson being basically free is another decent option if you need a 3rd TE.

- New York Jets: It is super easy to get starting QB Geno Smith late, and if you think he is dog water still, can take a shot on newly drafted Kade Klubnik in the final round for Jets stacks. After Breece Hall and Garrett Wilson, Omar Cooper Jr. is decently priced and should be used immediately but still lies a quite ambiguous situation behind those guys. Some nice late options include Braelon Allen (already flashing in OTAs!), AD Mitchell, Mason Taylor (Sadiq sports hernia surgery, but should be ready for camp), and could even take a stab at Tim Patrick, who they just signed to a 1-year deal and could be a one-week spike week guy as a RZ threat.

- Cleveland Browns: Either Brown's QB who wins the starting job are currently free in drafts between veteran QB Deshaun Watson and 2nd-year QB Shedeur Sanders. Browns also drafted Denzel Boston out of Washington (already getting major OTA hype), veteran WR Jerry Jeudy still on the roster for majorly cheap, Dylan Sampson presents a 3rd down role and contingent value behind Judkins, and you can also get Isaiah Bond (3-year, $3m) basically free. All of these options are essentially available after pick 160.

BIGGEST NFL News (OTAs) 5/26:

- As expected, 3rd-year TB RB Bucky Irving is NOT present on the field at OTAs as he is still recovering and rehabbing on injured shoulder that required surgery. HC Todd Bowles when asked about Irvings return: "Sometime in the summer or fall". 😕
- TEN QB Cam Ward on 1st-Rd Rookie Carnell Tate after first week of OTAs: "He'll have an explosive year just because he'll get a lot of one-on-one matchups. That's what we want. We want guys to come down and play, man, because we think we can beat him." 🚀
- 3rd-Year HOU TE Cade Stover has begun taking reps at FB while also serving as TE depth for the Texans. The Ohio State product has yet to play a full season due to injuries and is looking for a way to remain on the roster after cut-down time competing with the likes of Brevin Jordan, Foster Moreau, and Harrison Bryant.
- RUMOR MILL: Could Veteran SF WR Brandon Aiyuk be a low-risk, high-reward match to the New York Giants, who could possibly be without Malik Nabers to begin the 2026 season?
-  NY Jets want to give rookie QB Cade Klubnik a "good look and evaluation" before making any final decisions on signing a back-up veteran QB behind starter Geno Smith.
- Bear's QB Coach J.T. Barrett on emphasis for year three for starting QB Caleb Williams: "More efficiency, Less Superhero Plays". 
- Pelissero expects "fair competition going in" for the Minnesota QBs Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy heading into training camp. “If this were just, it’s Kyler’s job, and J.J. is competing with Carson Wentz to be the two, you would have traded J.J."
- Per @AlbertBreer: Chiefs, Chargers, Bills and Rams all had their shot at this already at AJ Brown and are all likely out." Stating the obvious also, what is most likely scenario is a reunion with Mike Vrabel.
- Raider's HC Klint Kubiak reiterates once again after second day of OTAs pertaining to 2nd year stud RB Ashton Jeanty on a potential snap split in the backfield: "I don't know the play snap percentage, but you look at Christian McCaffrey, his play snap percentage is high. So, those great backs, they don't want to come off the field." 🔥
- QB Lamar Jackson is indeed on the field and present in the Raven's 2nd week of voluntary OTAs after not being present last week. 
- Per Broncos Beat pertaining to Veteran TE Evan Engram, who is on the final year of his contract: "suspect he’s not on their roster by the start of training camp". The Broncos can save up to $6.7m against the cap (post-June 1st) by cutting Engram, and they did draft two TEs to boot.
- New Commander's OC David Blough to model new offense off of Bear's HC Ben Johnson scheme. Says QB Jayden Daniels "will be under center more frequently than any point in his career". Will utilize more pre-snap motion - Players like Terry McLaurin, Antonio Williams and Chig Okonkwo all thrive when put in motion, so the new scheme could be a good fit for their skill sets. 👀
- CAR RB Jonathon Brooks participating and looks to be a full go in early OTAs, reports are that he has "bulked up" a little and is already flashing nicely on run and passing game.
- Denver HC Sean Payton was asked "Who is the WR1 between veteran Courtland Sutton and newcomer Jaylen Waddle?", his response: "We’re not just going to have. So, we don’t have [that]. We just play different packages and try to give those guys the things that they do well.”
- The Houston Texans and star WR Nico Collins agreed on a contract negotiation/adjustment that guarantees Collins' salary for the next two NFL seasons, while also getting a pay increase of $8m for this season and $9m for next season. 
- 2nd-Year Jag's WR/CB Travis Hunter is indeed participating in Jaguar's OTAs this week. Was seen doing something quite unspeakable today - catching a pass on a WR route from QB Trevor Lawrence. We thought he only played CB now.
- Jacksonville HC Liam Coen was asked about why newly signed RB Chris Rodriguez Jr. was NOT in attendance for first day of OTAs: "Yeah, you know I mean, the injury report will come out at some point." 
- Per @Schultz_Report, Arizona Cardinals have fielded calls from teams around the league pertaining to trade discussions centered around Pro-Bowl DE/EDGE Josh Sweat.
- 3rd-year TB WR Jalen McMillan on excitement of opening the playbook under new OC Zac Robinson: "We had a lot of plays last year [under Josh Grizzard] that weren't called, but right now we are repping a lot of them and giving the coaches confidence in calling them."
- Jaguar's OC Grant Udinksi gushes over WR Parker Washington in Post-OTA interview: "I think really, really highly of Parker. I hope Parker thinks really, really highly of himself, because we think that he is capable of what he did at the end of the season last year and even more than that." 🚀

one of my favorite moments of winning on the margins in Villa's Europa final:

Freiburg score 40% of their goals on set pieces. Major corner threat with their height

Other clubs would pack the box with defenders on corner. Villa did the opposite. Placed four attackers near midfield

That left 6 Freiburg attackers vs 6 defenders and Emi Martinez, who is excellent at coming out for crosses

That led to a fast break roll out, with Youri on the ball and 4 other Villa attackers vs 4 retreating Freiburg defenders - which did not include the CBs that were up for the corner

This entire action was the beginning of Villa's second goal just before half, as they were able to keep to ball in that section of the field until Buendia's curler

Gadsden was a total liability as a blocker

1 on 1s. Couldn't hold down pin/pull. Even in split flow, working across the formation. Whiffed on fill players and second-level targets
